Part of the "Korean Musical ON SCREEN" cinema series, which brings masterpieces of Korean musical theater to the big screen, this installment features the 2018 premiere production of the original musical "The Man Who Laughs."

England, late 17th century. Gwynplaine, a boy whose mouth was mutilated to be used as a sideshow attraction, finds a dying baby named Dea in the snow and encounters Ursus, a traveling performer living in a nearby hut. Years later, the grown Gwynplaine has become known as "The Man Who Laughs" due to the scars on his mouth. Alongside the blind Dea, he becomes a celebrity by performing his own life story in plays under Ursus's guidance. One day, an aristocrat takes an interest in their show, causing the fates of Gwynplaine and Dea to shift dramatically.

Based on the novel "The Man Who Laughs"—which Victor Hugo, the literary giant behind "Les Misérables," considered his own masterpiece—the production features a book and direction by Robert Johanson, lyrics by Jack Murphy, and music by Broadway hitmaker Frank Wildhorn. Following its 2018 world premiere in South Korea, the musical was also staged in Japan in 2019 and 2022, where it drew widespread acclaim for its delicate human drama and epic score.
title:小屋番 八ヶ岳に生きる 劇場版: This documentary journeys through the Yatsugatake mountains alongside mountain photographer Tetsuo Kikuchi, capturing the untold stories of those who confront nature and life through stunningly beautiful cinematography. Based on "KOYABAN: Living in Yatsugatake," which premiered at the TBS Documentary Film Festival 2025, this theatrical release has been re-edited with new interviews and footage of the changing seasons. In a region so dense with mountain huts it is nicknamed "Koyagatake," the film turns its lens toward the "koyaban" (hut keepers) who have chosen this path. It explores why they embrace a harsh daily existence facing nature head-on without the comforts of cars or convenience stores, and delves into the gravity of their work—supporting climbers and, at times, encountering death.
title:おててつないで:
Actor Yuki Sugawara took on a total of 26 roles—including director, screenwriter, producer, cinematographer, and lead actress—to complete this medium-length film. Drawing on over a decade of experience as a bridal photographer and film crew member, Sugawara channels the "regret of failing to be honest with loved ones" she felt after her father’s death into this story of a distorted parent-child relationship, told with a touch of surreal humor.

Yoshihisa Sunaga, a nursing assistant, lives with his 31-year-old daughter, Yui. Their relationship is strained, with Yui maintaining a cold, rebellious attitude toward him. One day, Yoshihisa is fired following a blunder at work. While Yui views the situation with detached optimism, Yoshihisa’s circumstances grow increasingly dire. Though they have lived under the same roof for years, the father and daughter know next to nothing about each other. When Yoshihisa finally reveals a secret to Yui, their lives are forced to change.

Veteran actor Kiyofumi Kaneko, known for the *Midnight Diner* series, stars as the awkward Yoshihisa, while Sugawara herself plays his distant daughter. The film was an official selection for the "A Certain Viewpoint" category at the 26th TAMA NEW WAVE in 2025.
tmdb:1427396:
Directed by Katsuyuki Motohiro of the "Bayside Shakedown" series, this youth sci-fi comedy is a film adaptation of the popular stage play by the theater group Europe Kikaku. Set during a sweltering summer vacation, the story follows a group of college students who trigger a series of chaotic events when they use a time machine to retrieve an air conditioner remote before it broke.

In the clubroom of a university's Sci-Fi Research Society, five male students and two female members of the photography club are languishing in the oppressive heat. Their air conditioner's remote control broke the previous day, leaving them to suffer through the summer. Suddenly, a time machine appears before them. What starts as a joke turns into a discovery: the machine is real. Caught up in the excitement, three members decide to travel back to yesterday to fetch the working remote. However, a junior from the year 2030 suddenly appears before those left behind, leading the group to realize the grave dangers of altering the past...

The screenplay was penned by Europe Kikaku’s own Makoto Ueda. Eita, who rose to prominence in "Water Boys," makes his film-starring debut as Komoto, the student at the heart of the commotion. Juri Ueno, known for "Swing Girls" and "Turtles Are Surprisingly Fast Swimmers," plays Shibata, the photography club member and object of Komoto's affection.
title:愛のごとく:
"Ai no Gotoku" (Like Love) is the posthumous short story by Masao Yamakawa, a brilliant figure of post-war Japanese literature, originally published in 1964. Director Kishu Ido, renowned for his adaptations of literary classics such as "Manji" and "A Fool's Love," brings this exploration of human nature—caught between love and solitude—to the screen for the first time.

Hayao, a man who debuted as a novelist but now earns a living as a writer, happens upon a couple immersed in a world of SM one night. Despite being bound by her husband, the wife locks eyes with Hayao and offers a smile—a sight that leaves a deep impression on his soul. Soon after, he reunites with his former lover, Izumi, for the first time in eight years. As reality, memory, and fantasy intertwine, Hayao’s heart begins to waver, drawing him back toward his past with Izumi.

Robin Furuya, known for the drama "VIVANT," makes his feature film starring debut as Hayao. Reimi Miyamori ("My Head is Always Noisy") portrays Izumi, supported by a cast including Mami Yamasaki, Mutsuo Yoshioka, Miyoko Yoshimoto, and Chizuru Azuma. The screenplay is penned by Kaori Kotani, marking her third collaboration with Director
title:GRIT バレーボール男子日本代表 栄光への始発点: A documentary film documenting the 2025 challenge of the Japan Men's National Volleyball Team. Despite their valiant efforts at the 2025 World Volleyball Championship—the culmination of the 2025 season—the team failed to reach the final tournament and was eliminated in the preliminary round. This film follows the team closely throughout the season, from the national team call-up in May
tmdb:1437156:
This feature film serves as the sequel to the 2023 television drama *Last Man: The Blind Investigator*, which followed a blind FBI agent and a solitary detective as they teamed up to tackle a series of complex cases.

FBI Special Agent Hiromi Minami (Masaharu Fukuyama) lost his sight in a past incident, yet earned the moniker "Last Man" for his uncanny ability to bring every investigation to a definitive close. During an exchange program with the Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department, he was assigned an attendant in Shintaro Godo (Yo Oizumi), a lone-wolf detective who stops at nothing to make an arrest. After forging an invincible partnership and discovering the shocking truth that they are biological brothers, the two were bound by an even deeper bond before Minami eventually returned to the United States.

Two years later, Minami returns to Japan and summons Godo to join him on a case in Hokkaido. There, they encounter Nagisa Ivanova (Rie Miyazawa), Minami’s first love. Teaming up once again to protect her from a mysterious organization, Minami and Godo join forces with a joint task force comprising the FBI, CIA, and Hokkaido Prefectural Police. However, lurking behind the case is a conspiracy capable of shaking the world.

The film features returning cast members Ren Nagase, Mio Imada, Tae Kimura, Yo Yoshida, and Takaya Kamikawa, with Rie Miyazawa joining the ensemble as the pivotal character Nagisa Ivanova.
tmdb:1559125:
Director Eisuke Naito, who has sharply depicted the harsh realities and struggles of teenagers in films such as *Liverleaf* and *Forgiven Children*, presents a monster panic adventure set in the unique situation of young people caught in illegal "dark labor" facing off against a ferocious brown bear.

On the very day 18-year-old Osanai receives his university acceptance letter, his father commits suicide after falling victim to a specialized fraud scheme that left him with massive debt, forcing Osanai to abandon his plans for higher education. In his desperate search for money, Osanai finds himself stepping into the world of "dark labor"—the very underworld that drove his father to the brink. One day, Osanai participates in a kidnapping operation promising a high reward, only to find that the job has escalated to include the murder of the abducted woman and the disposal of her body. Just as they carry the woman into the forest and are about to commit the deed, an unprecedented situation occurs.

Fuku Suzuki stars as the protagonist Osanai, while Wan Marui (*Backfire*, *The Unknown Girl*) takes on her first action role as his highly skilled combat partner, Sakurako Wakabayashi. Additionally, Takashi Ukaji plays the key role of Hunter Kanzaki, and special effects/makeup artist Tomo Hyotake, who also worked on *Golden Kamuy*, handled the design of the brown bear.
tmdb:1605972: A heartwarming comedy that became a massive hit in France, depicting the chaos that ensues when a father-and-son jewel thief duo hide out at a summer camp for people with disabilities. Paulo and his father rob a jewelry store, but when their getaway car is towed, they are left with no means of escape. Stranded on the street, they are mistaken for new arrivals by staff from a care facility about to depart for a summer camp. Taking advantage of the confusion, they pose as a resident and his caregiver to sneak into the camp. The lively days spent with the vibrant residents gradually begin to soften the hearts of the father and son. Popular French comedian, actor, and writer Artus serves as director and screenwriter while starring as Paulo, joined by veteran actor Clovis Cornillac (Paris 36) as the father. The fellow campers are played by 11 amateur actors who actually have disabilities.
tmdb:1591447:
The popular anime series "Girls & Panzer," which depicts high school girls dedicating their youth to "Senshado" (the Way of the Tank)—a fictional martial art involving tank warfare—is receiving an anime adaptation of its official spin-off manga, "Girls & Panzer: Motto Love Love Sakusen desu!" This film marks the first act of a four-part theatrical series.

Miho Nishizumi and the members of the Oarai Girls' Academy Senshado team have been fully committed to the Way of the Tank, from their victory in the 63rd National High School Senshado Tournament to the fierce battle against the University Selection Team, where students from various schools united to save Oarai from closure. However, they are still high school girls at heart. Away from the heat of practice and matches, they fully enjoy a peaceful and fun-filled school life. Embracing the spirit of "yesterday's enemy is today's friend," they engage in lively "cross-cultural" exchanges with students from schools like Anzio High School, St. Gloriana Girls' Academy, Chi-Ha-Tan Academy, and Kuromorimine Girls' Academy. But as they are drawn to the unique cultures and charms of these other academies, one student after another begins to contemplate transferring...

The film is directed by Masami Shimoda (Zegapain series), with series composition and screenplay by Noboru Kimura (Code Geass: Rozé of the Recapture). Animation production is a joint effort between P.A.WORKS, known for hits like SHIROBAKO, and Actas, the studio behind the Girls & Panzer series.
title:シネマ歌舞伎 歌舞伎NEXT 朧の森に棲む鬼 松也版:
"Kabuki NEXT: Oboro no Mori ni Sumu Oni" (Demons in the Oboro Forest), which was performed at the Shinbashi Enbujo in November and December 2024 and at the Hakata-za in February 2025, is now coming to the big screen as part of the "Cinema Kabuki" series. Based on Shakespeare’s *Richard III*, the production is a kabuki reimagining of the Gekidan Shinkansen play *Oboro no Mori ni Sumu Oni*, which depicts a man at the mercy of his own lies and desires. The stage production featured a double-cast lead, with the protagonist Rai played by Matsuya Onoe (who starred in Shinkansen’s *Metal Macbeth*) and Koshiro Matsumoto (who starred in the original Shinkansen version under his former name, Somegoro Ichikawa). Cinema Kabuki will release both versions.

Driven by insatiable greed, Rai swindles women and money with his silver tongue. In the forbidden Oboro Forest—a place where those who enter are said to become monsters—he strikes a forbidden pact with the forest’s demons: in exchange for his own lifeblood, they will grant him the throne. While Rai steadily ascends toward the crown through a trail of sacrifice, his rise also signals his inevitable destruction.

This "Matsuya Version" features Matsuya Onoe in the lead role of Rai, supported by a cast including Bando Yajuro, Nakamura Tokizo, Bando Shingo, Onoe Ukon, Ichikawa Somegoro, and Matsumoto Koshiro. As with the original Shinkansen production, the script was written by Kazuki Nakashima and directed by Hidenori Inoue.
title:シネマ歌舞伎 鰯賣戀曳網: The 37th installment of the "Cinema Kabuki" series, which brings digital screenings of Kabuki stage performances to movie theaters. This film captures the January 2009 stage production at the Kabuki-za, performed as part of the "Kabuki-za Farewell Performances" with a cast led by Nakamura Kanzaburo and Bando Tamasaburo. The play is the second Kabuki work written by Yukio Mishima, following "The Hell Screen." Sarugenji, a sardine seller, falls in love at first sight with the high-ranking courtesan Hotarubi after spotting her at Gojo Bridge. Smitten, he loses the vigor in his signature sales cry. His father, Ebina Naamidabutsu, devises a plan to disguise Sarugenji as a feudal lord and the horse trader Rokurozaemon as his chief retainer so they can go to meet Hotarubi. With Kanzaburo as Sarugenji and Tamasaburo as Hotarubi, a stage filled with laughter unfolds. In addition to the main feature, the screening includes a special interview with Tamasaburo.
title:A DOCUMENTARY FILM OF LOSTAGE ひかりの: A documentary exploring the enigma and allure of the alternative rock band LOSTAGE as they mark their 25th anniversary. Formed in 2001 and based in Nara, the three-piece band continues to perform at live houses across Japan. Despite choosing a path that avoids major distribution and the hit charts, every one of their releases has sold over 5,500 copies, with sales of their back catalog continuing to grow. Now in their 40s, the three members have built families in Nara and run community-based businesses, maintaining a musical career that is inextricably linked to their daily lives. Once burdened by the expectation to "make it big" and having experienced a major label debut, they reached a turning point in 2011, navigating a winding path through the shifting realities of life ever since. Directed by filmmaker MINORxU, who shares a 20-year history with the band, the film depicts the trajectory of three men who are revered by fellow musicians yet live grounded lives as everyday people.
title:最初の年 民意が生んだ、社会主義アジェンデ政権: The feature-length directorial debut released in 1972 by Chilean master Patricio Guzmán, renowned for documentary works such as *The Battle of Chile* trilogy and *Nostalgia for the Light*. In 1970, Salvador Allende was elected as the first socialist president in Chilean history, signaling the start of a nationwide social transformation. This film meticulously captures the tumultuous first year of his administration from the perspective of the people. Guzmán recorded voices in mines, rural villages, ports, cities, and schools, reflecting the impact of government policies on daily life, the public's sense of elation, and their spirit of participation in the revolution through imagery brimming with hope and energy. Meanwhile, the camera also documents ominous signs, including friction with existing power structures, economic chaos, and political instability. Following the 1973 military coup in Chile, many prints were lost, and the film became a legendary "lost" work. However, it has been resurrected through a restoration process spanning half a century under Guzmán’s supervision, with the 2K restored version making its world premiere at the Anthology Film Archives in New York in September 2023. It will have its first theatrical release in Japan in November 2025.
tmdb:1613385:
The classic drama series "Oretachi no Tabi," a quintessential symbol of youth in the Showa era, receives its first-ever film adaptation. Since its debut as a serial drama on the Nippon TV network in 1975, the series has continued through special episodes produced at various milestones in the protagonists' lives. This sequel, arriving 20 years after the last installment, marks the directorial debut of lead actor Masatoshi Nakamura.

Kosuke Tsumura (nicknamed "Kasuke"), his college classmate Takao Kanzaki ("Omeda"), and his elementary school senior Shinroku Kumazawa ("Guzu-roku") have now reached their 70s, their friendship spanning over 50 years. Kasuke runs a small local factory, Omeda serves as the mayor of Yonago City in Tottori Prefecture, and Guzu-roku is the chairman of a nursing care facility—each living out their peaceful daily lives.

One day, Omeda visits Kasuke's factory, but he appears deeply troubled
title:石炭の値打ち:
A two-part social drama produced in 1977 by British master Ken Loach for the BBC’s "Play for Today" anthology series. Scripted by Barry Hines following their 1969 collaboration *Kes*, the film is set in a coal mine—a workplace that symbolized British society at the time—and meticulously portrays the lives and struggles of those who live there.

Part 1, "Meet the People" (77 min), follows a mining town ahead of an official visit by the Prince of Wales. With humor and irony, it depicts the residents scrambling to perform "staged" cleaning and repairs, and workers being mobilized solely for the sake of appearances. Through the authorities demanding superficial decorum and the workers at their mercy, the film exposes the structural absurdity and inherent violence of a class-based society.

Part 2, "Back to Reality" (91 min), takes a sharp turn toward a hard-hitting, serious tone, poignantly depicting a tragic accident born from the disregard for workers' rights and a negligent management system. It offers a raw look at the reality of miners working on the brink of death and the families forced to face the aftermath of tragedy.

Long difficult to view in Japan due to the lack of home video or streaming releases, the film will receive its Japanese theatrical premiere in November 2025.
title:ヒポクラテスの盲点: A documentary that explores the impact of COVID-19 vaccine aftereffects from a multifaceted perspective. Five years have passed since the government issued the first state of emergency during the spread of the coronavirus, and two years have gone by since its classification under the Infectious Diseases Act was downgraded to Class 5. While national infectious disease measures have evolved through this unprecedented crisis, reports have begun to surface regarding the damage caused by aftereffects of the COVID-19 vaccine (mRNA gene product)—a new technology distinct from conventional vaccines. The approval and distribution of this unprecedented "new drug" encapsulated deep-seated issues within Japanese society: not only medical blind spots and limitations but also data manipulation designed to obscure inconvenient facts, and the anticipatory compliance of academia and the media toward the government. Amidst these circumstances, a group of doctors sought to scientifically investigate the impact of these vaccine aftereffects. By capturing the perspectives of people from diverse backgrounds—including patients, bereaved families, and vaccine proponents of the time—the film aims to unveil the truth based on science and facts.
tmdb:4972: Popular Danish actor Mads Mikkelsen stars in this Danish black comedy about the struggles of two men who find themselves serving human meat to their customers. After opening their own butcher shop, Sven and Bjarne discover the body of an electrician who had been accidentally locked in their freezer. Through a bizarre turn of events, Sven prepares a marinade using the corpse’s meat and serves it to a customer, only for the dish to become an unexpected sensation. Desperate to maintain the shop’s reputation, he begins killing people one after another to procure more "ingredients." Mikkelsen delivers an eccentric performance as the town outcast Sven, sporting a unique hairstyle, while Nikolaj Lie Kaas of the "Department Q" series plays his partner, Bjarne. The film is written and directed by Anders Thomas Jensen, who has collaborated with Mikkelsen on numerous works, including "Adam's Apples." In Japan, the film will receive its first theatrical release in November 2025 as part of the special screening project "The Treasure of Scandinavia: Mads Mikkelsen's 60th Anniversary Festival."
title:最後の賭け: Screened as part of "Eiga no Kuni Masterpiece Selection II: Claude Chabrol Unreleased Masterpieces," a series showcasing previously unreleased works in Japan by the French New Wave master who passed away in 2010. The film depicts the fate of a pair of con artists who steal money from the mafia, captured through diverse location filming ranging from the snowy mountains of Switzerland to the tropical Antilles. The lead duo is played by Isabelle Huppert (*The Piano Teacher*) and Michel Serrault (*The Children of the Marshland*).
title:プシュパ 覚醒: A massive Indian blockbuster depicting the turbulent life of a man who rises through the ranks of the high-end timber smuggling trade and the fierce conflicts that erupt along the way. Pushpa, a young man working for a syndicate smuggling red sandalwood—a rare wood native only to South India—lives a life of poverty, oppressed by his half-brothers and forbidden from using a surname due to his status as the son of a concubine. Vowing to one day make a name for himself, Pushpa manages to outsmart the police and successfully transport red sandalwood worth hundreds of millions of rupees. His feat earns him the respect of the three Reddy brothers who lead the organization, and he is promoted to become the right-hand man of the eldest brother, Konda Reddy. Having finally acquired wealth, Pushpa decides to marry Srivalli, the woman he loves. However, his jealous half-brothers interfere, exposing the truth of his birth to his fiancée’s family. Directed by Sukumar (*Rangasthalam*) and starring Allu Arjun (*Ala Vaikunthapurramuloo*). The film was originally released in Japanese theaters in October 2023 as part of the "Hot Wind!! The World of South Indian Cinema" special screening lineup. With the sequel *Pushpa 2: The Rule* set for a January 2026 release, this film will return to theaters for a limited one-week revival screening.
tmdb:1537112:
A documentary by journalist Yasunori Kawakami, covering Palestine and Israel following the events of October 7, 2023. On that day, the militant group Hamas launched a cross-border attack from the Gaza Strip—a Palestinian territory blockaded by Israel’s "separation wall"—prompting the Israeli military to launch a devastating retaliatory offensive "beyond the wall." With the death toll exceeding 50,000, including more than 18,000 children, the situation is catastrophic, and as there are still no signs of a ceasefire, the number of victims continues to rise. 

Amidst conditions making it difficult for foreign journalists to enter Gaza, Director Kawakami traveled to the Palestinian West Bank—also separated by a wall—in July 2024 to conduct field reporting. Traveling from Bethlehem to Hebron and on to Masafer Yatta—the setting of the film *No Other Land*—he witnesses firsthand the destruction caused by Israeli military operations and the violence perpetrated by Jewish settlers. Meanwhile, on the Israeli side, while much of the public remains unaware of the devastation beyond the wall, three young people stand firm in their refusal to perform military service. By vividly capturing the realities on both sides of the wall, the film brings the complex background of the Israel-Palestine conflict into sharp focus.
tmdb:86241: Composer Carol returns from Europe to Los Angeles, where her wealthy father, Carl, lives. Carol becomes involved with various women... An ensemble drama set in Los Angeles, depicting the tangled romantic entanglements and loneliness of various men and women. Directed by Alan Rudolph and produced by Robert Altman, with whom Rudolph collaborated on *Nashville* and *Buffalo Bill and the Indians*.
title:医の倫理と戦争: This documentary delves into the issues facing modern medicine while tracing the dark history of medical professionals' complicity in war crimes. At the root of the various problems plaguing Japan's contemporary medical landscape lies the reality of medical professionals' participation in World War II war crimes and the subsequent cover-up. Doctors belonging to "Unit 731," led by Shiro Ishii, transformed the "findings" gained through human experimentation into personal achievements, rising to the center of Japan's post-war medical establishment. Confronting this dark history, there are medical professionals who continue to speak out against war under the banner of "medical ethics." This film uncovers the truth of Unit 731 while documenting the current efforts of medical professionals as they tackle various issues. Directed by Sosuke Yamamoto (*Monshen*).
tmdb:85474: Portuguese visionary director Pedro Costa, acclaimed for *In Vanda's Room* and *Colossal Youth*, follows the musical career of French actress Jeanne Balibar (*My Sex Life... or How I Got into an Argument*, *Va savoir*) in this documentary. From vocal lessons and album recordings to rehearsals and live concerts, the film moves from France to Tokyo, capturing Balibar’s multifaceted expressions through stunning black-and-white cinematography.
tmdb:53016:
The third installment of the "War Trilogy" by Roberto Rossellini—the standard-bearer of Neorealism who profoundly influenced the directors of the New Wave—this drama depicts the cruelty of war through the lives of a young boy and his family in the ruins of post-WWII Berlin. Utilizing Neorealist techniques such as extensive location shooting and the use of non-professional actors, the film portrays the tragedy that befalls the boy with a documentary-like touch.

Berlin after the fall of Nazi Germany. Young Edmund lives in a cramped, rented room with his frail, bedridden father; his brother, a former Nazi Party member who hides indoors for fear of the police; and his sister, who supports the household while nursing their father. To earn money in place of his father and brother, Edmund wanders the devastated city instead of attending school. One day, he reunites with Enning, his former primary school teacher. Having been expelled from the school system and now involved in the black market, Enning preaches Nazi ideology, by which the innocent Edmund gradually becomes influenced.
title:臆病者:
A drama by Indian master Satyajit Ray, based on a short story by writer Premendra Mitra, depicting the clash of new values in an Indian society standing at the crossroads of tradition and modernization. Amit, a screenwriter living in Kolkata, experiences a car breakdown while on a research trip in the countryside and ends up staying at the home of Bimal, a tea plantation owner he meets by chance. There, he is reunited with Karuna—the woman he once loved but left due to his own cowardice, and who is now Bimal’s wife. While Amit attempts to make amends for the past, Karuna remains composed, her true feelings unreadable. Before his departure, Amit tells Karuna to come to the station if she still loves him, but...

The film stars Ray regular Soumitra Chatterjee as Amit, with Madhabi Mukherjee, who also appeared alongside Chatterjee in Ray’s previous work *Charulata*, playing the role of Karuna. In its native India, the film was released as a double feature with Ray’s *The Holy Man*. In Japan, it will make its theatrical debut in July 2025 as part of the "Satyajit Ray Retrospective 2025," a special screening program commemorating the 70th anniversary of the director's debut.
tmdb:572979: A poetic and humorous road movie depicting a young girl’s journey to Bosnia to visit her estranged father and the two young men who become her traveling companions. Alma is a Bosnian girl living in the Netherlands. Her parents fled their war-torn homeland to raise her there, but her father eventually left Alma and her mother to return to Bosnia, leading to years of estrangement. When news arrives that her father has been hospitalized, Alma follows her mother’s wishes and sets off for Bosnia alone. At the airport, she is met by her cousin Emir, who is surly and unhelpful; only Denis, Emir’s self-proclaimed “intern,” is willing to listen to her. Alma boards a bus for the town where her father is staying, but during a rest stop, the bus leaves her behind, driving off with only her luggage. Bosnian-born, Dutch-raised Ena Sendijarević makes her feature directorial debut, projecting her own roots onto the protagonist while drawing significant influence from Jim Jarmusch’s *Stranger Than Paradise*. The title is derived from a song by the Scottish rock band Mogwai. The film won the Special Jury Award in the Tiger Competition at the 48th International Film Festival Rotterdam in 2019.
title:在日ミャンマー人 －私たちの自由－: On February 1, 2021, a military coup took place in Myanmar. Commander-in-Chief Min Aung Hlaing seized full power and assumed leadership of the nation. The military invalidated the 2020 general election and declared a state of emergency, detaining Aung San Suu Kyi and other leaders of the National League for Democracy (NLD), the party that had won the election. In response to the coup, citizens across Myanmar fiercely resisted through non-violent protests. Many civil servants left their posts to join the Civil Disobedience Movement (CDM) as a sign of resistance. However, the military responded with a violent crackdown, resulting in numerous casualties, including deaths from torture. As a result, an estimated 3.5 million people or more fled the military's oppression, becoming internally displaced persons. In Japan as well, many of the approximately 40,000 Myanmar residents living in the country at the time rose up in protest immediately following the coup. Young technical interns working across the country gave up their days off to travel from rural areas to join protests in Tokyo, while also working tirelessly to raise funds for support efforts on the ground.
title:ガザからの声 Episode2 「ミュージシャン・アハマドのメッセー: Ahmad Abu Amsha is a musician and a father of five. He once earned his living performing concerts. Originally a resident of Beit Hanoun in northern Gaza, he has been displaced 12 times since the start of the war and currently lives in a camp within the Unknown Soldier's Square in western Gaza. When a ceasefire was announced in January 2025, he returned to Beit Hanoun, only to find his home and studio reduced to rubble. Nevertheless, he remained there, pitching a tent inside a room that was still relatively intact. In March 2025, following the collapse of the ceasefire, he moved to Al-Rimal, where he resides today.
title:在日ミャンマー人 わたしたちの自由:
Director Toshikuni Doi, who helmed the 2012 documentary "Living in a Foreign Land: Burmese in Japan"—which followed Kyaw Kyaw Soe, an exile who fled to Japan for the Myanmar democratization movement—returns with this sequel focusing on young Myanmar residents in Japan. On February 1, 2021, a military coup occurred in Myanmar, with Commander-in-Chief Min Aung Hlaing seizing power. The military invalidated the previous year’s general election and detained leaders of the victorious National League for Democracy, including Aung San Suu Kyi. While the public resisted through non-violent protests, the military’s brutal crackdown resulted in numerous casualties and over 3.5 million internally displaced persons. In Japan, many of the roughly 50,000 Myanmar residents began staging protests immediately after the coup. Young technical interns from across the country flocked to Tokyo to join the demonstrations and worked tirelessly to raise funds for support back home.

The first part of the film captures the raw emotions of the youth participating in these protests and provides an update on Kyaw Kyaw Soe’s life. The second part follows a Myanmar woman in Japan who supports a school for children displaced to towns along the Thai border. The third part investigates the Japan Myanmar Association, which is rumored to have connections with the military.
tmdb:1599751:
Poet Chise Yamanaka pens her first screenplay for this medium-length film, depicting the reunion of two sisters who had lost touch after leaving their seaside hometown. Ku-chan, the elder sister, has lost her sense of purpose amidst the monotony of city life when she receives an unexpected phone call from her younger sister, Saki-chan. Choosing to meet at a beach away from their childhood home, a poem they once read together serves as a catalyst, awakening shared memories and emotions. In a world where memory and dreams blend and reality and imagination intertwine, the sisters embark on a new journey of "escape."

Based on Yamanaka’s original script titled after her poem "In the Midst of Escape" and incorporating excerpts from her "Dream Diary," the film explores the collaboration between cinema and language. It frames the sisters' reunion as a metaphor for the reconciliation of "fragmented words" and "the body," weaving a fresh perspective on the shared experience of time and space.

Yukino Murakami (*August My Heaven*) stars as Saki-chan, while Marino Kawashima (*Floating Boundary*) plays Ku-chan. The film was produced by Kinokoya, a cafe-bar and cine-club in Tama, Tokyo, and directed by Yukinori Kurokawa (*Garden Sandbox*, *Village on the Village*).
tmdb:1600743: Into the home of Japanese sisters Kameko and Tsukiyo Hinomoto, two illegal immigrant sisters from Largo—Forte and Voce—come barging in...
title:シケモクとクズと花火と:
Set in Hiroshima Prefecture, this mysterious youth drama depicts the encounter and journey of self-discovery between three individuals of different ages, backgrounds, and values. Sueko Sugiyama, a part-timer at the Hiroshima izakaya "Mamesuke," leads a lackluster life with no dreams or romantic prospects, venting her daily frustrations by bitterly critiquing those around her and society at large. Her world is disrupted by the arrival of a new part-timer, Sakuto Yamane. A university student and handsome influencer with exceptional social skills, Sakuto secretly feels a sense of emptiness behind his bravado and superficial relationships. With polar opposite personalities and worldviews, Sueko and Sakuto clash from the moment they meet. However, their lives take a bizarre turn when they accidentally witness a group of elderly people in wheelchairs lynching a young man. As they flee in confusion, a mysterious girl named Ako appears before them.

The film stars Yumi Endo (*For Family*) as Sueko, On Nakano (*BLUE FIGHT*) as Sakuto, and Miu Mori (*Teacher Maria’s Revenge Counseling*) as Ako, with veteran actress Chizuru Azuma co-starring as the izakaya’s proprietress. Directed by Kentaro Yamagishi—known for the short film *Tokyo Stateless Girl*, which was later adapted into a feature by Mamoru Oshii—the screenplay is penned by Sakiko Yashige (*30,000,000*).
title:マスター 先生が来る！: Starring Indian superstar Vijay, known for "Bigil" and "Jilla," this action drama follows an unconventional teacher who confronts a ruthless gang to reform delinquent youths. JD, a professor at a prestigious university, is popular among his students for his toughness and proactive nature. However, after a riot breaks out during a student union election he advocated for, he is forced to take responsibility and resign. JD then takes up a new post at a juvenile detention center in a provincial town, only to find the facility controlled by a local gangster named Bhavani, creating a wretched environment where the boys are mired in crime and violence. JD stands up to fight for the boys' reformation. Vijay stars as JD, with acclaimed actor Vijay Sethupathi playing the role of Bhavani. The film is directed by Lokesh Kanagaraj, the director of "Kaithi."
title:ガザからの声 Episode2「ミュージシャン・アハマドのメッセージ: The second installment of "Voices from Gaza," a documentary project that brings to the world the reality of the Gaza Strip in 2025 and the voices of those living there—truths that remain hidden behind fragmented news clips and social media videos. Ahmad Abu-Amsha, a musician and father of five, lived in Beit Hanoun in northern Gaza but has been forced to evacuate 12 times since the start of the war. Following a ceasefire announcement in January 2025, he returned to Beit Hanoun only to find his home and music studio reduced to rubble. In March of the same year, as fighting resumed and his life of displacement continued, Ahmad resolved to support the spirits of his community through music. Alongside fellow musicians, he began teaching instruments and song to children to help ease the anxieties of war. For him, music is a form of resistance and the most powerful way to project his voice to the world. Sending a message to artists worldwide that "silence in the face of injustice is complicity," Ahmad continues to play, aiming to bring joy to as many children as possible. This film is a joint project between Gaza-based production company Aleph Multimedia and Tokyo-based distributor Uplink; Aleph conducted the local filming and reporting, while Uplink handled editing, distribution, and the theatrical release. The film will be released simultaneously in theaters and online.
title:WHO？: A documentary depicting the light and shadow of a society struck by the COVID-19 pandemic. On September 28, 2024, a large-scale demonstration took place in Ariake, Tokyo. Banners, placards, and T-shirts worn by participants bore scathing criticisms of the government-recommended vaccines and the WHO. While similar large-scale demonstrations were held across the country that year, the mainstream media failed to report on their full scope. Through interviews with the protesters, the film sheds light on why they were driven to demonstrate and the specific issues they are raising. Directed by Yusei Naruse, known for "You Are Still in a Long Tunnel" and "Immigrants Under the Floorboards."
title:落語家の業:
A documentary depicting the life of Rakugo performer Kairakutei Black. A disciple of the legendary Danshi Tatekawa, Black is known for a self-destructive lifestyle and radical Rakugo style that seems to embody his master’s famous words: "Rakugo is the affirmation of human karma." Despite limited media exposure, he has maintained a loyal following for many years. In an era of strict compliance, he is a "true entertainer" the likes of whom may never appear again; his philosophy of laughing off every misfortune offers a way to navigate today’s suffocating world. 

Born in 1952 to a U.S. soldier and a Japanese woman, Black spent much of his childhood in the darkness of movie theaters to escape discrimination. He became an apprentice to Danshi Tatekawa in 1969 but was expelled after it was discovered he had used his master’s money for horse racing. He returned to the fold in 1979, took the name Kairakutei Black II, and was promoted to *shin'uchi* (master rank) in 1992. However, in 2005, he voluntarily left the Tatekawa school after a debt of 20 million yen came to light. While continuing to draw crowds with his provocative Rakugo, his deep knowledge of Kabuki and Japanese cinema has also led to a career as a critic and screenwriter. The film is directed by Kyosuke Enokizono, who moved into filmmaking after organizing film festivals and managing movie theaters. Narration is provided by *katsudo benshi* (silent film narrator) Raiko Sakamoto.
tmdb:1023: A human drama written and directed by Anders Thomas Jensen, the acclaimed screenwriter of *Open Hearts* and *Brothers*, starring Mads Mikkelsen (*The Hunt*). Drawing motifs from the Old Testament’s "Book of Job" and the fable of Adam’s apples, the film uses dark humor to depict the eccentric characters inhabiting a rural church. Adam, a neo-Nazi skinhead on parole, is sent to a countryside church that doubles as a rehabilitation center. When his mentor, the priest Ivan, asks him to set a goal, Adam dismissively replies that he will bake an apple cake using fruit from the garden. The residents of the church are all peculiar, including Ivan himself, who uses blind faith to shield himself from a harsh reality. As Adam relentlessly attempts to strip away Ivan’s self-deception, a series of bizarre calamities begins to plague him. Ulrich Thomsen (*In a Better World*) stars as Adam, with Mikkelsen portraying Ivan.
title:甘い罠: Screened as part of "Eiga no Kuni Masterpiece Selection II: Claude Chabrol’s Unreleased Masterpieces," a series showcasing previously unreleased works in Japan by the French New Wave master who passed away in 2010. Based on American author Charlotte Armstrong’s *The Chocolate Cobweb*, this psychological crime drama explores the conflict between good and evil lurking in the depths of the human heart, set to the strains of classical masterpieces including Liszt’s piano piece "Funérailles."
title:フジコ・ヘミング 永遠の音色:
A documentary about the world-renowned pianist Fuzjko Hemming, who passed away in April 2024 at the age of 92. Capturing the hearts of audiences both in Japan and abroad with her emotional and dynamic performances, she was known as the "Pianist of the Soul." Director Soichiro Komatsu, who has documented her life for 12 years—including in the 2018 film *Fuzjko Hemming: A Pianist of Silence & Solitude*—delves into her true character, portraying her soul-stirring music and way of life. 

The film features performance scenes including her breathtaking "La Campanella," numerous classical masterpieces, and rare original compositions, some of which are being premiered for the first time. It also includes interview footage of Fuzjko herself, her illustrated diaries, and testimonies from her actor brother Wolf and her half-sister Eva, whose existence is revealed in this film. The documentary looks back on her turbulent life: overcoming the childhood separation from her Swedish father, her mother’s rigorous lessons, the hardships of being stateless, a life of poverty while studying abroad, unforgettable love, and the loss of her hearing, before finally gaining recognition and becoming a beloved pianist in her 60s. Narration is provided by Miho Kanno, who portrayed Fuzjko in the 2003 special drama *The Trajectory of Fuzjko Hemming* and was a close personal friend.
title:アリス（1988）: The debut feature film from visionary Czechoslovakian animator Jan Švankmajer. Based on Lewis Carroll’s novel *Alice's Adventures in Wonderland*, the film brings to life a surreal and grotesque world through a unique fusion of live-action and stop-motion animation. While passing a tedious afternoon in a cluttered room, a young girl named Alice begins playing "Alice" with her dolls. Suddenly, a taxidermied White Rabbit displayed in a glass case springs to life. Checking its pocket watch in a frantic rush, the rabbit vanishes into a desk drawer. Alice follows, and before she knows it, she has wandered into a bizarre and unsettling world.
title:時のおと:
Actor and filmmaker Toru Katayama, who gained international acclaim with his 2019 feature debut *Roar*, presents a cinematic poem filmed over the course of a year across the four seasons in five towns of his native Fukui Prefecture. Centered on local dialects and the philosophy that "a town is defined by its sounds," the film offers a sincere portrayal of the people living there.

A high school girl facing her final summer in the drama club; a woman captivated by the sounds of the town, trying to make time stand still; a fisherman confronting an inevitable generational shift; and a man who moved to the area to grow vegetables while waiting for spring. The sounds of daily life they produce are gentle and quiet, yet unique to each location—sounds that resonate with the memories of every viewer.

The film stars Shiori Ueno (*The Legend of the Strongest Hitman Kunioka*), Utano Aoi (*Time Machine Girl*), Nami Sasaki (*Bachiranun*), Tamaki Kubose (*Randen*), and veteran actor Kanji Tsuda. Produced with the full cooperation of Fukui City, Obama City, Minamiechizen Town, Sabae City, and Katsuyama City, it also features many real-life residents of these communities.
title:SEX:
The first installment in a trilogy by Norwegian director Dag Johan Haugerud—a former bestselling author and librarian—this film explores three perspectives on "romance," "love," and "sex" set in the Norwegian capital of Oslo. With a comedic touch, it depicts two married men as they reconsider their "masculinity" following unexpected experiences.

The two men, both chimney sweeps with families, find their lives disrupted. One discovers a new sense of excitement after an unexpected sexual encounter with a male client; however, his relationship with his wife becomes strained when he tells her about the experience without a hint of remorse. The other begins to wonder how his own identity is shaped by the gaze of others after dreaming that David Bowie perceives him as a woman. Having lived as "good fathers" and "good husbands," both men must now confront their own sense of masculinity and identity.

The film won three awards at the 74th Berlin International Film Festival in 2024, including the Ecumenical Jury Prize. In Japan, it will be released in theaters in September 2025 as part of the special screening series "Oslo: Three Landscapes of Love," alongside the other entries in the trilogy, "LOVE" and "DREAMS."
title:DREAMS:
Norwegian director Dag Johan Haugerud—a former bestselling author and librarian—presents a drama exploring the lives of three generations of women with differing values, centered on a young girl’s candid memoir about her love for a female teacher. Set in the Norwegian capital of Oslo, the film is the final installment of Haugerud’s trilogy chronicling three perspectives on "sex," "love," and "desire." At the 75th Berlin International Film Festival in 2025, it became the first Norwegian film ever to win the Golden Bear, the festival's top honor.

Seventeen-year-old Johanne, experiencing her first love for her teacher Johanna, records her yearning and exhilaration in a personal memoir. Hoping to share her feelings, Johanne shows the writing to her grandmother, a poet, but the situation takes an unexpected turn. While the grandmother is reminded of her own history of struggle as a woman, Johanne's mother attempts to categorize the work through a modern lens as a "feminist novel documenting a lesbian awakening."

The film will be released in theaters in September 2025 as part of the special screening series "Oslo: Three Landscapes of Love," alongside the trilogy’s previous two installments, *SEX* and *LOVE*.
title:ヒア・アンド・ゼア:
A human drama from up-and-coming Spanish director Antonio Méndez Esparza that realistically portrays family bonds and the underlying sense of loss through the eyes of a father returning home after years of working abroad. Pedro, a Mexican man who has been working in the United States, returns to his home village for the first time in several years. Though bewildered by the emotional distance from his family and the changing ways of the village, he attempts to slowly rebuild his connections with them—but...

Featuring a cast largely composed of actual villagers, the film is told in a unique style where fiction and documentary intersect. The songs in the film were written and performed by Pedro himself. The film won the Grand Prix at the 65th Cannes Film Festival’s Critics' Week in 2012. In Japan, following a screening in the "WORLD CINEMA" section of the 25th Tokyo International Film Festival in 2012, it will receive its first theatrical release in 2025 as part of the special program "The Films of Antonio Méndez Esparza."
title:リー・ミラー 彼女の瞳が映す世界:
Starring and produced by Kate Winslet, this film dramatizes the extraordinary life of Lee Miller, the real-life figure who transformed from a top fashion model into one of the 20th century's most iconic photojournalists. In 1938, while vacationing in the South of France with a circle of artist friends, Lee meets and falls in love with the artist Roland Penrose. Before long, the looming threat of World War II changes their lives forever. Securing work as a photographer, Lee teams up with David Scherman, a photojournalist and editor for the American magazine *LIFE*. In 1945, serving as a war correspondent and photographer, Lee captures a series of historic scoops. On the day of Hitler's suicide, she signals the end of the war by photographing a portrait of herself in the bathtub of Hitler's Munich apartment. The sights she witnessed remained etched in her mind, haunting her for many years after the war.

Kate Winslet’s performance as Lee Miller earned her a Golden Globe nomination for Best Actress in a Motion Picture – Drama. The film features a supporting cast including Marion Cotillard as Lee's friend Solange d'Ayen, Alexander Skarsgård as her lover Roland Penrose, and Andy Samberg as David Scherman. Ellen Kuras, the acclaimed cinematographer of *Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind*, makes her feature directorial debut.
title:サイレントナイト:
A film noir starring veteran actor Shun Sugata, who also provided the original story. Set against the scenic backdrop of Otaru, Hokkaido, the film depicts a man who has spent his life in the underworld fighting to protect a fragile love.

Keizo Kuno once belonged to the world of the yakuza. Now living quietly in a corner of Otaru after abandoning his past, he sends annual Christmas gifts to Ayaka—the daughter of his former underling, Mitsumura—under the guise of being her uncle. Ayaka, a high school student, lives a peaceful life despite suffering from a memory disorder. With graduation approaching, Ayaka visits Kuno—believing him to be her mother’s brother—to ask about the whereabouts of the mother she barely remembers. In reality, Ayaka is Kuno’s biological daughter, a truth he has been unable to reveal for a specific reason. Amidst this, a conflict in the underworld intensifies due to Kuno’s past ties, forcing him to take a stand to protect Ayaka’s future.

Yuka Yano, selected through an audition, plays the role of Ayaka, joined by a supporting cast including Sosuke Takaoka, Shigemitsu Ogi, Hidekazu Akai, Atsushi Arai, Daisuke Nagakura, Kohei Otomo, Tetsu Watanabe, Miyoko Yoshimoto, and Sanae Miyata. Director Kenichi Fujiwara, who previously teamed up with Shun Sugata for *Korou no Mure*, helms the film.
title:主人公: Directed, written, and scored by the Indian master Satyajit Ray, this drama skillfully critiques the issues within the Bengali film industry through the crisis and introspection of a movie star. Arindam, a popular actor in Bengali cinema, is scheduled to attend an awards ceremony in Delhi, but after failing to secure a flight, he makes the journey by sleeper train. On board, he is recognized by all the passengers, including an eccentric old man who disapproves of his drinking habits and a mother and daughter who are fans. During the trip, Arindam is interviewed by Aditi, a female journalist who happens to be on the same train. Through their conversation, he reflects on his life and confronts the loneliness and inner conflict hidden behind his glamorous success. Uttam Kumar, a legendary star of the Bengali film industry at the time, plays the lead role of Arindam, while Sharmila Tagore (The World of Apu) portrays the journalist Aditi. In Japan, the film will make its theatrical debut in July 2025 as part of the "Satyajit Ray Retrospective 2025," a special screening series commemorating the 70th anniversary of the director's debut.
title:六つの顔:
Director Isshin Inudo (*Josee, the Tiger and the Fish*, *The Floating Castle*) presents a documentary following Living National Treasure and Kyogen master Nomura Mansaku. A leading figure in Kyogen—a traditional Japanese performing art with a history of over 650 years—Mansaku continues to take the stage even today, with a career spanning more than 90 years. Following his receipt of the Order of Culture in 2023, a commemorative performance was held in June 2024, where he performed "Kawakami," a masterpiece he has polished as his lifework.

The film follows him through that special day of performance while employing a bold yet delicate approach to explore his artistic realm, using animation to depict the "six faces" Mansaku envisions when reflecting on his past. In addition to Mansaku himself speaking on his devotion to Kyogen, the film features interviews with his son, Nomura Mansai, and grandson, Nomura Yuki. Furthermore, the camera captures the majestic, primordial scenery of Kongo-ji Temple in Kawakami Village, Nara—the setting of "Kawakami"—bringing Mansaku’s long-pursued worldview to the screen alongside his supreme artistry.

The animation is handled by Koji Yamamura, who was nominated for an Academy Award for Best Animated Short Film for *Mt. Head*, with narration by actor Joe Odagiri. Nomura Mansaku and Nomura Mansai served as supervisors for the film.
title:ハリー・スミス作品集: Harry Smith (1923–1991) was a singular talent who pioneered the psychedelic era, traversing not only creative fields like painting and film but also American roots music research and cultural anthropology. This program screens *Number 11: Mirror Animations*, an alchemical animation work of freely collaged cut-out materials; *Number 14: Late Superimpositions*, a pioneering superimposed film layering footage of New York butcher shops and the Kiowa people; *Number 15*, featuring close-ups of North American Seminole patchwork; and *Number 19*, restored by Anthology in 2025.
title:「イメージレス・フィルムズ」シリーズより: In the realms of avant-garde and experimental cinema, filmmakers have repeatedly challenged the medium by exploring how many elements considered essential and indispensable to the definition of film can be stripped away, and whether a work can still function as cinema in their absence. Featuring films that emphasize dialogue or sound collage, works where visual elements consist primarily of text, projector performances manipulating light and darkness, and flicker films, this program explores how artists have experimented with the possibilities of "imageless" cinema—or, more broadly, the concepts of "void" and visual subtraction.
title:クッチャー兄弟作品集: Twin brothers Mike (1942–) and George Kuchar (1942–2011) were based in New York from the 1960s through the 1970s, releasing low-budget films that incorporated camp aesthetics and queer representation. *Hold Me While I'm Naked* is George Kuchar’s masterpiece, depicting the anguish of a sexually frustrated filmmaker; it was also selected for The Village Voice’s "100 Best Films of the 20th Century." *Sins of the Fleshapoids* is Mike Kuchar’s first solo directorial effort, a comic-book-style sci-fi film that influenced John Waters. *I, An Actress* is one of many short films George Kuchar produced while teaching at the San Francisco Art Institute, capturing the screen test of an aspiring actress.
title:｢アヴァンギャルド広告｣シリーズより: The field of cinema preserved and screened by Anthology has long been categorized as "experimental," "avant-garde," or "independent," sparking ongoing debate regarding the validity of such terms. In particular, the label "non-commercial"—carrying the connotation of resistance against the commercialism epitomized by Hollywood—has been widely regarded as an all-encompassing descriptor. However, the "Avant-Garde Advertising" series, held at Anthology starting in November 2025, sheds light on instances where even filmmakers embodying purity and radicalism produced sponsored works with promotional intent, prompting a redefinition of these fundamental distinctions. Are the works presented here a form of experimentation, an avant-garde incursion into enemy territory, a reflection of the reality that everyone must work to earn a living, or perhaps all of the above? Through this program, questions are raised that remain open to a multitude of answers.
title:武田純一郎作品集: Following the suspension of production at the former Tokyo Cinema, Junichiro Takeda established Cine Science (now ICAM) in 1968, going on to produce numerous scientific films utilizing microscopic photography. This program features three works: *The History of Rats Written in Chromosomes*, which reveals the history of rats as a species through chromosome analysis; *From Egg to Human*, which traces the mystery of the birth of life accompanied by narration from sisters Eriko and Kyoko Kishida; and *The Wonder of Holes*, which employs both microscopic and outdoor photography to unravel the functions of various holes, pores, and tubes of all sizes.
title:久里洋二 短篇アニメーション作品集: Yoji Kuri pioneered the possibilities of Japanese independent animation with his stark, razor-sharp lines and a boundless imagination fueled by absurdity and eros. Throughout the 1960s and 70s, he collaborated with avant-garde luminaries from various fields, including musicians Toru Takemitsu, Toshi Ichiyanagi, and Isao Tomita, and the poet Shuntaro Tanikawa. This program presents a collection of his award-winning works from international animation festivals—masterpieces that sent shockwaves through the global creative community.
tmdb:1494120: The first installment of a nine-part series that became the life’s work of Jin Tatsumura, who produced a wide range of films following his tenure at NHK’s News and Education Bureau. Through the poetic words of six individuals who achieved greatness in their respective fields—including Reinhold Messner, the legendary climber of the world’s 8,000-meter peaks—this epic documentary explores the harmony between humanity, nature, and the Earth as a singular, vast living organism. It sounds a warning against the crises brought about by the relentless advancement of science.
title:田名網敬一作品集: A leading figure in Japan’s underground art scene, Keiichi Tanaami has worked prolifically across a wide range of genres, including painting, collage, sculpture, animation, experimental film, and installation. This program screens eight of his works that influenced Japanese experimental cinema, produced by drawing on the methodologies and techniques he cultivated as a designer.
title:ショージとタカオ: Shoji Sakurai and Takao Sugiyama were convicted of a 1967 robbery-murder in Fukawa, Ibaraki Prefecture (the Fukawa Incident) and released on parole after 29 years in prison. Director Yoko Ide, who honed her documentary filmmaking skills under Sumiko Haneda, stays close to these two victims of a miscarriage of justice, thoughtfully portraying how they rediscover the joys of life.
tmdb:1561767:
Masaharu Fukuyama directs his second live film, capturing the "Great Freedom" free concert held in his hometown of Nagasaki in October 2024.

On October 13, 2024, the Japanet Group presented "Great Freedom" as the grand opening performance for Nagasaki Stadium City. From over 530,000 applicants, 25,000 fans gathered at the main venue in a whirlwind of excitement. Combined with free broadcasts at adjacent sites and live viewings hosted by municipalities across Nagasaki Prefecture, more than 310,000 people witnessed the historic event.

Fukuyama himself supervised every aspect of the production, including the direction, visuals, and sound. Captured from a 360-degree perspective using 52 cameras, the performance has been reconstructed for the cinema with cutting-edge audio technology and stunning visual beauty, bringing the immersive atmosphere of the venue to the big screen. Centered on the concert footage performed under the moonlight, the film weaves an emotional narrative by interspersing scenes of Fukuyama’s childhood—a time before he had even begun to dream of his future.

Following the previous film, *FUKUYAMA MASAHARU LIVE FILM: Kotodama no Sakiwau Natsu @ NIPPON BUDOUKAN 2023*, actor Hinata Hiiragi—who played the younger version of Fukuyama’s character in the TV drama *Last Man: The Blind Investigator*—returns to portray the artist in his youth.
title:BE:the ONE START BEYOND DREAMS:
The third installment in the "BE:the ONE" documentary series follows the popular dance and vocal group BE:FIRST on their first-ever world tour, held from April to July 2025.

Spanning 12 cities across Asia, Europe, and North America, the "BE:FIRST World Tour 2025 Who is -BE:FIRST?-" transcended language and culture to captivate audiences worldwide. This film features not only their stunning live performances but also the moments of inspiration the members found in various cities and their brief moments of downtime. Furthermore, because the members themselves took turns behind the camera, the film offers an even more authentic, true-to-life look at the group.

The documentary also focuses on RYOKI, who went on hiatus (and subsequently left the group) following the Singapore performance on July 5. It portrays the feelings of each member and their experiences on the ground, capturing their final performance as a seven-member group and their interactions backstage. Additionally, the film includes brand-new interviews capturing the raw emotions of the members after returning home, delving deep into where they stand today.
tmdb:1600737: Actor Ageha Tanaka is suddenly swept into a journey across the multiverse. Alongside Kanai, an aspiring film director, and Alien K, a traveler driven by curiosity, Ageha embarks on a strange odyssey. Amidst their travels, he finds himself reminiscing about the days spent with his late best friend, Glico.
tmdb:1497303:
Advertising balloons in the sky, billboards on the ground—the world is truly in the golden age of promotion. Advertising agencies are constantly pitching one startling idea after another to their sponsors. At Heart Advertising, a small firm, their rising star Ichimura is so busy delivering a passionate pitch for a department store account that he completely forgets his date with his girlfriend, Masako. When they finally manage to embrace in the shade of a park tree and their lips are just about to meet, Ichimura suddenly shouts "That's it!" and tosses her aside, struck by a killer catchphrase. Naturally, Masako’s cheeks puff up with more indignation than a giant advertising balloon.

But an even bigger shock awaited Masako, who has already been waiting three years for a proposal: Ichimura holds a televised wedding with a woman named Mayumi Nakamura. Standing beside Ichimura as he recites his vows in formal morning dress is a woman in a wedding gown, blushing and gazing at him dreamily. Of course, this was just another of Ichimura’s promotional stunts, but when Mayumi starts hiccuping at the critical moment, the broadcast becomes a total disaster! Unaware it was a stunt, Masako breaks down in tears,
title:両親が決めたこと:
STORY
Claudia (80), a stage actress in Barcelona, is suffering from terminal cancer. As the cancer metastasizes to her brain, bringing with it the threat of confusion, partial paralysis, and the loss of her sense of self, she chooses euthanasia. Claudia has always lived her life prioritizing the stage over motherhood. Her husband, Flavio, has supported his charming actress wife throughout her career and remains deeply in love with her. Bound by an eternal bond, the couple decides to undergo euthanasia together and reveals their plan to their three children. The children do not agree with their father’s decision, and the eldest daughter goes as far as to accuse her mother of manipulation, leading to a confrontation. However, the father’s resolve is firm. As the parents proceed with the necessary steps for their joint euthanasia, the departure for their final journey suddenly draws near...
title:笑えない世界でも:
I hope no one empathizes with this film.

When I found myself completely alone, I watched a certain TV drama (*Seija no Koushin*, written by Shinji Nojima) and wept, realizing that it was okay for me to be alive. From that moment, Atsushi Okamoto aspired to become a film director, driven by a desire to create and deliver works that could offer even the slightest solace to those who find life difficult to endure. This is his first original feature film, delicately tracing the inner lives of two young girls.

Ayaka, making her starring debut as a "loner" girl working without hope in a small factory, won the Best Actress Award at the 10th Kashikojima Film Festival for her performance. Playing the other protagonist—another isolated soul on the brink of despair—is Misaki Tanifuji, who graduated from an idol group in 2023 and appeared as the Hakata gal "Tamatchi" in the 2024 NHK morning drama *Omusubi*.
title:パンダのすごい世界:
Nominated for Best Documentary/Educational Film at the 38th Golden Rooster Awards—the most prestigious honor in Chinese cinema.

With their iconic black-and-white markings, round figures, and endearing behavior, giant pandas have captivated hearts worldwide. In Japan, they have been cherished for half a century since their first arrival in 1972.

This documentary takes an intimate look at the life of the panda. Following pandas of various ages living in Sichuan Province, Beijing, and Hong Kong, the film portrays their journey from birth and growth to rewilding training, international cultural exchange, and their twilight years. In addition to revealing little-known aspects of their daily lives, the film explores breeding and conservation efforts, as well as the 8-million-year history of the panda, often referred to as a "living fossil." It is a valuable visual record capturing the unique ecology of these extraordinary creatures.
title:大統領暗殺裁判 16日間の真実:
Based on historical events, this suspense film centers on the trial following the 1979 assassination of South Korean President Park Chung-hee by the Director of the KCIA. It depicts three men caught in the whirlwind of events between the assassination on October 26, 1979, and the military coup on December 12 of the same year.

Jung In-hu, an ace lawyer who stops at nothing to win, takes on the defense of Park Tae-ju, the chief secretary to the KCIA Director who was involved in the assassination following orders from his superior. As a military man, Park is tried alone in a court-martial, where a final verdict is set to be delivered a mere 16 days after the first hearing. However, it soon emerges that the trial is being unfairly manipulated by Chun Sang-doo, the head of the Joint Investigation Team and the central figure of the massive power that would eventually orchestrate the military coup.

Jo Jung-suk (Hospital Playlist) stars as the protagonist Jung In-hu. Lee Sun-kyun (Parasite), who passed away in December 2023, plays Park Tae-ju in what became his final film. Yoo Jae-myung, known for Itaewon Class and The Solitary Gourmet Movie, portrays Chun Sang-doo. The film is written and directed by Choo Chang-min (Masquerade).
tmdb:1372044: This documentary chronicles the struggle of Iwao Hakamada, who spent 47 years on death row. Convicted for the 1966 murder-arson of a miso company executive’s family in Shizuoka Prefecture, Hakamada was suddenly released in March 2014 after 47 years and seven months of imprisonment. Arrested at the age of 30 after spending his youth as a professional boxer, his pleas of innocence were ignored by both the courts and the public. He endured decades of terror, living each day with the fear that his execution might be carried out the next morning, and was 78 years old by the time of his release. Amidst dramatic media coverage of the unprecedented decision to release a death row inmate pending a retrial, director Chiaki Kasai—who has followed Hakamada for 22 years—records the story behind the headlines. The camera captures the daily lives of Hakamada and his sister, Hideko, reunited after being torn apart for nearly half a century, and through their dialogues, delves deep into Hakamada’s inner psyche.
title:私は死にたくない:
Directed in 1958 by Robert Wise, the master behind *West Side Story* and *The Sound of Music*, this black-and-white social drama is based on the true story of Barbara Graham, a woman who maintained her innocence until the very end despite being sentenced to death for robbery and murder.

Barbara had left her life of prostitution behind after getting married. However, years later, struggling with poverty and her husband’s drug addiction while raising a child, she is forced back into the life to survive. When a robbery-homicide occurs, she is arrested as a suspected accomplice for being seen with the men involved. Interrogated under the presumption of guilt, Barbara desperately pleads her innocence, but her cries fall on deaf ears...

Susan Hayward (*Rawhide*, *I’ll Cry Tomorrow*) stars in a powerhouse performance that earned her the 31st Academy Award for Best Actress, among many other accolades. The film co-stars Theodore Bikel (*The Enemy Below*) and Simon Oakland (*Psycho*). It is featured in the special screening series "Shinjuku Hardcore Masterpieces" (July 18 – September 4, 2025, at Cinemart Shinjuku), a retrospective showcasing the masterpieces and unconventional works of America’s legendary directors.
title:SAYAMA みえない手錠をはずすまで: This documentary follows Kazuo Ishikawa and his wife, Sachiko. Ishikawa was convicted of the 1963 "Sayama Incident"—the murder of a high school girl in Sayama City, Saitama—through a forced confession and fabricated evidence, and he continues to maintain his innocence to this day. Having endured 32 years in prison and 19 years on parole, Ishikawa has lived with the stigma of being a murderer for over half a century. Despite his petitions for a retrial being repeatedly rejected, he and Sachiko continue their fight for justice. Through their unwavering journey and their perspective that they were "unlucky, but not unhappy" despite a lifetime of hardship, the film conveys universal messages about the beauty of life, happiness, love, friendship, and justice.
title:ジュンについて:
This documentary follows Junichiro Shimada, the man behind Natsuhasha, a one-person publishing house based in Kichijoji, Tokyo. Founded in 2009 with the mission to create "books to be read over and over again," Natsuhasha publishes about three literary titles a year, ranging from reprints of forgotten Showa-era masterpieces to essays by bookstore owners. From editing and accounting to packing and shipping, Shimada handles every aspect of the business alone. Despite starting with no prior experience in publishing, he has sustained this work for 15 years.

Shimada spent a difficult youth struggling to find his place; he aspired to be a writer until age 27, but after facing setbacks and failing to adapt to conventional employment, he felt adrift. His life finally began to move forward following the death of his cousin in his hometown of Kochi. Finding solace in a single poem during his grief, he decided to publish it himself as a gift for his cousin’s parents. That book, *After the Farewell*, went on to become Natsuhasha’s signature publication. Having once been saved by books himself, Shimada now seeks to give back to the world of books, bookstores, and the communities that surround them.

Filmed over 580 days, the documentary captures Shimada’s daily life intertwined with books. Director Ryutaro Tano (*Children’s Summer: Chernobyl and Fukushima*) personally handled everything from production to distribution, with actor Shohei Uno providing the narration.
title:カルテットという名の青春:
This documentary offers an authentic look at the youthful days of four young musicians as they charge full speed toward their dreams. Originally broadcast on BS Asahi in 2011 and recipient of the 49th Galaxy Award, the program has been re-edited for this theatrical release. It carefully portrays the emotional nuances of the four musicians—now active on both domestic and international stages—during their formative years.

Music students Taro Uemura (violin), Madoka Sakitsu (violin), Mariko Hara (viola), and Dai Miyata (cello), whose talents were recognized early on, formed the Jupiter Quartet Japan. Through intense practice and the collision of their passions and talents, they grew to be hailed as "Japan’s finest young quartet." With high confidence, they entered the ARD International Music Competition in Munich, the most prestigious challenge for any quartet, only to face elimination in the first round. Experiencing a major setback for the first time in their lives, the four set out on a journey to find a sound that could truly resonate with the world.

Following them over a period of four years, the film captures the radiant days of young people devoting themselves wholeheartedly to their craft. Actor and singer Tomoyo Harada serves as the narrator.
title:太陽（2005）: Russian visionary director Alexander Sokurov, known for *Spiritual Voices* and *Russian Ark*, presents this provocative film as the third installment in his tetralogy exploring 20th-century leaders, following *Moloch* (Hitler) and *Taurus* (Stalin). The film depicts the internal conflict of Emperor Showa, who was revered as a deity, leading up to his "Humanity Declaration" at the end of the war. Issey Ogata stars as the Emperor, alongside Kaori Momoi as the Empress and Shiro Sano as the Grand Chamberlain.
title:チェチェンへ アレクサンドラの旅: Director Alexander Sokurov (*The Sun*, *Taurus*) presents a profound human drama filmed on the actual front lines of the Chechen conflict. World-renowned Russian opera singer Galina Vishnevskaya makes her cinematic debut, delivering a masterful performance as the protagonist, Alexandra. Eighty-year-old Alexandra travels to the front lines of the war in Chechnya to visit her grandson, Denis. Concerned for her grandson, a soldier whose profession is to kill, Alexandra...
title:青春: Acclaimed Chinese director Wang Bing, world-renowned for documentaries such as *Tie Xi Qu: West of the Tracks*, *Three Sisters*, and *Dead Souls*, observes the lives of young people working in small garment factories within one of China's massive economic zones. The film is set in the Yangtze River Delta, a region centered around Shanghai that has long sustained China’s rapid economic growth. Focusing his camera on anonymous workers in their late teens and twenties in the town of Zhili, Wang Bing records the realities of their labor and daily existence. In addition to screening in competition at the 76th Cannes Film Festival in 2023, the film won Best Documentary at the 60th Golden Horse Awards and the Douglas Edwards Experimental Film Award at the 49th Los Angeles Film Critics Association Awards.
title:バレンタイン一揆: Child labor is a serious issue in Ghana, Africa, a region renowned for producing the cocoa used in chocolate. This documentary follows three ordinary young Japanese people who, upon learning the truth, travel to Ghana’s cocoa-growing regions to take action against child labor. University student Kotoko Fujita and high school students Maho Umeda and Arika Shiga met through ACE, an international NGO dedicated to the abolition and prevention of child labor. Visiting Ghana as representatives of the ACE Youth Academy, they witness the harsh reality of children forced to work on cocoa plantations instead of attending school. Moved by their experience, the three organize "Valentine Ikki" (Valentine Uprising), an initiative held on Valentine’s Day—when chocolate consumption is at its peak—to encourage people to choose fair-trade chocolate.
title:やさしさ: A featured film in the "Central Asia Past and Present Film Festival Vol. 3: Uzbekistan Special."
title:苦い果実: A film screened at the Central Asia Past and Present Film Festival Vol. 3: Uzbekistan Special.
title:トゥギャザー:
A horror film that thrillingly depicts a couple in a stagnant relationship pushed to their limits by supernatural physical mutations, infused with a healthy dose of black humor. Marking the feature debut of Australian director and screenwriter Michael Shanks, the film blends the body horror subgenre with themes of romantic codependency.

Long-time partners Tim, an aspiring musician, and Millie, an elementary school teacher, leave the city to start a new life in a house in the countryside. However, after getting lost in the woods and spending a night in an eerie underground cave, their peaceful routine takes a dark turn. Tim begins to suffer from mysterious symptoms—sudden lapses in consciousness and a body that acts of its own accord—straining his already drifting relationship with Millie. Soon, Millie begins to experience the same symptoms. Drawn together as if by an invisible magnetic force, the phenomenon begins to erode the love they’ve built and every aspect of their lives.

Dave Franco (*Now You See Me*) and Alison Brie (*Promising Young Woman*) star as Tim and Millie. Real-life partners, the duo brings a raw realism to this exploration of the deep-seated psychology of love.
title:METライブビューイング2025-26 R・シュトラウス《アラベッラ:
The third production of the 2025–26 season of "MET Live Viewing," which brings the latest performances from New York’s Metropolitan Opera (MET) to movie theaters. This installment features Richard Strauss’s *Arabella*, an opera set to a libretto by the great Austrian writer Hugo von Hofmannsthal that depicts a tale of love woven into the aristocratic society of 19th-century Vienna (scheduled to be recorded from the performance on November 22, 2025).

Vienna, circa 1860. The family of the retired Count Waldner is on the verge of bankruptcy. Lacking the funds for marriage dowries, they have disguised their younger daughter, Zdenka, as a boy, while pinning their hopes on their eldest daughter, Arabella, to marry into wealth. Arabella, however, remains indifferent to her many suitors, including Zdenka’s friend Matteo. When Arabella finally accepts a proposal from the wealthy landowner Mandryka, a heartbroken Matteo is driven to despair. Zdenka, who is secretly in love with Matteo, grows concerned and hands him the key to her own room, claiming it is the key to her sister’s. Overhearing this, Mandryka becomes convinced he has been betrayed and flies into a jealous rage.
title:『小屋番 八ヶ岳に生きる 劇場版』舞台挨拶付き上映: Yatsugatake is home to so many mountain huts that it is often nicknamed “Koyagatake.” Join mountain photographer Tetsuo Kikuchi as he journeys through these peaks to meet the “hut keepers”—individuals who have chosen this path for reasons all their own. In a world without convenience stores or cars, what led them to choose a grueling daily life of facing nature head-on?
title:不貞の女: A suspense thriller from French master Claude Chabrol, a leading figure of the New Wave, starring his wife at the time, Stéphane Audran. Charles, an insurance executive, lives a happy life with his wife Hélène and their son, but one day he happens to catch Hélène answering a suspicious phone call. Suspecting her of infidelity, Charles hires a private detective and discovers that her lover is a writer named Pégala. Charles visits Pégala’s home to meet his wife's lover face-to-face. Though he initially maintains a facade of composure, he is gradually overcome by jealousy and rage... Highlights include the sophisticated visual beauty captured by cinematographer Jean Rabier (known for Agnès Varda’s *Le Bonheur* and Jacques Demy’s *The Umbrellas of Cherbourg*), the performances of a distinguished cast, and delicate psychological portrayals.
