Previously unreleased in Japanese theaters, the film will make its domestic theatrical debut as part of the "Claude Chabrol Masterpiece Selection" (starting February 13, 2026, at Cinema Rise, Morc Asagaya, and other venues), a retrospective featuring three of Chabrol’s suspense films released between the late 1960s and 1970.
title:女鹿: Directed by Claude Chabrol, a leading figure of the Nouvelle Vague known for films such as *Les Cousins* and *The Third Lover*, this suspense drama explores the mysterious relationship and the web of love and hate woven by two contrasting women. The story begins when Frédérique, a wealthy bourgeois woman, becomes infatuated with Why, a young woman she spots drawing on the pavement of the Pont des Arts over the Seine. Frédérique is assertive, flamboyant, and lives a life of leisure on her father’s inheritance; Why is a quiet, struggling aspiring artist. Despite their opposite natures, Why—weary of her impoverished life—accepts Frédérique’s invitation to live at her villa. However, when Why finds herself drawn to Paul, an architect attending one of their parties, Frédérique notices and decides to seduce him herself, sparking a tense affair. Frédérique is played by Stéphane Audran, who was married to Chabrol at the time, appearing here alongside her former husband Jean-Louis Trintignant. The film earned Audran the Silver Bear for Best Actress at the 18th Berlin International Film Festival.
title:私のすべて:
A French drama that delicately portrays the emotional and physical liberation of a woman who has devoted her life to her son. Mona, a single mother living in the suburbs of Paris, has raised her son Joel, who has developmental delays, alone since she was young. Now in his early 30s, Joel works at a vocational workshop for people with disabilities. Mona and Joel have spent their lives supporting and caring for one another, but their world is upended when Océane, a coworker at Joel’s facility, becomes pregnant with his child. Having known nothing of their relationship, Mona is shaken, and the bond between mother and son begins to waver.

Laure Calamy (*Only the Animals*) stars as Mona, while the roles of Joel and Océane are played by Charles Peccia-Gallet and Julie Froger, both of whom are actors with disabilities. The production employed the French film industry’s only accessibility coordinator to support the actors with disabilities on set. Directed by Anne-Sophie Bailly (co-writer of *Dog on Trial*) in her feature debut, the film won the Authors Under 40 Award for Best Director in the Orizzonti section at the 81st Venice International Film Festival in 2024.
title:枯れ木に銃弾:
"Senior Noir"—a new genre that reimagines classic film noir in modern Japan through the eyes of the elderly.

*Bullets in Withered Trees* is a provocative work by Shinichiro Tsukasa, who is making his debut as a commercial film director at the age of 75. Though he aspired to be a filmmaker in his youth, family circumstances forced him to abandon his dream for
title:MOOSIC LAB2026:
【super໒꒱cute໒꒱fragile — Sawa Kawakami Director Special】
"December Rain" (23 min) ©︎ "December Rain" Film Partners

An inspired film based on the classic song "December Rain," originally released in 1970 on Happy End's self-titled debut album. An "inner world road movie" following Marin, a girl searching for her birthplace, as she travels through her hometown attempting to recover paintings.

Director, Screenplay, Editor: Sawa Kawakami
Cast: Marin Hidaka, Jun Hosoi, Yuriko Tanaka, Ayu (Taro Monogatari), Kanon (Taro Monogatari), Yui Nishide, Konnichi Hakase (Nankyoku), Tomomi Nakai (Voice)
title:あなたならどうする？ 人生を決める 百万ドルのキス: To reclaim her cherished family home, Van reluctantly follows a plan devised by her sister Kieu and best friend Tien to get close to two wealthy heirs pursuing her. One is a man of calm, mature charm and warmth; the other is young, bold, and a playboy. But just as Van realizes her true feelings, she discovers that both men had their own hidden motives for approaching her. Which one will ultimately receive Van’s "million-dollar kiss"?
title:Returnees（リターニーズ） 元こども兵 それぞれの再起: Set in Uganda, East Africa, this documentary follows former child soldiers struggling to reclaim their lives and the efforts of a Japanese NPO supporting them. For over 20 years, Uganda was gripped by conflict between the government and the Lord’s Resistance Army (LRA), an anti-government insurgent group. During the civil war, the LRA abducted more than 30,000 children, forcing boys into combat and girls into forced marriages with soldiers. The LRA continues to operate from neighboring countries, where they still carry out child abductions. In 2023, approximately 140 former child soldiers and their families escaped from these active conflict zones. Known as "returnees," these individuals were kidnapped as children, deeply traumatized, and robbed of their futures. Having grown up in brutal conditions, they lack formal education and struggle to meet even their most basic needs. Shingo Ogawa, a director of the NPO Terra Renaissance, has developed a three-year training program to support their reintegration, standing by them as they work to regain their self-esteem and find a place in society. Recording these efforts over a period of two years, the film captures the former child soldiers as they take their first courageous steps toward a new life.
title:JUST ANOTHER: A documentary following the punk band "the Genbaku Onanies" as they reached their 38th anniversary in 2020. Formed in Nagoya in 1982, the group centered on EDDIE, formerly of THE STAR CLUB, and TAYLOW, known locally as the "Punk Doctor." Through numerous lineup changes, the band is also known for having included members such as Tatsuya Nakamura (who later joined BLANKEY JET CITY) and Ken Yokoyama of Hi-STANDARD. Remaining committed to their home base in Aichi, they have held steady jobs rather than aiming for professional status, and continue to play punk rock today even after turning 60. In this film—the first documentary of their career—the members themselves reveal internal truths about the band that have never been told before. It is directed by Michiko Oishi, the filmmaker who created "MOTHER FUCKER," a documentary about the music label "Less Than TV."
title:劇場版 ほんとうにあった怖い話 ゾク変な間取り:
The second installment in the omnibus horror series "The Movie: True Scary Stories - Strange Floor Plans," which depicts inexplicable events occurring in houses with bizarre layouts.

Director Matsumura and cameraman Fujii are reviewing footage for a documentary following Rinko Soma, a "Seikashi" (House Organizer)—a professional who specializes in "arranging" homes. As Soma "loosens" the pillars and floors of a house where a gruesome incident once occurred, the camera captures something unexpected (Episode 1: "The House Organizer").

Okudera, who works for a real estate management company, tracks the whereabouts of a missing rookie employee named Mishima to a vacant house that has failed to find a buyer. On the floor plan of that house, suspicious marks—not standard legal symbols—were inscribed (Episode 2: "Site Investigation").

Having lost his fiancée Mari just before their wedding, Ohno spends his final moments in the room where they were supposed to share a peaceful life together... (Episode 3: "Newlywed Life").

Funyao Harada of the comedy duo "Danbi Ramucho," Koji Ito of "Lanjatai," and solo artist Sui star in each respective episode.
title:ヴァリドマン:
This original tokusatsu (special effects) film was produced by the Kuno Laboratory at Bunkyo Gakuin University, led by film producer and director Professor Tsutomu Kuno, whose research focuses on content creation, management, and marketing for industrial renovation. The project was driven by a group of kaiju-loving students passionate about the genre, who collaborated with professional staff, voice actors, and cast members to bring the film to life.

On the day of her father’s funeral, a young girl named Miku takes a part-time job traveling around Tokyo in a mascot costume of Bunkyo City’s character, "Kattan." However, the being inside Kattan is actually an ancient inhabitant of the lost continent of Mu. Sensing that the Fierce Beast Taifu—the creature that once destroyed Mu—is about to awaken from its seal, Kattan pleads with Miku to become the warrior Validman and fight. Just as Miku begins to bond with Kattan and his yokai followers, Taifu finally resurrects. Kattan grows to a giant size to engage in a fierce battle, but is ultimately defeated. In that moment of dire peril, Miku transforms into the giant hero Validman.

The voice of Kattan is performed by popular voice actor Tomokazu Sugita, famous for his roles as Gintoki Sakata in "Gintama" and Gyomei Himejima in "Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba." Chie Koujiro, known for "Obocchama-kun," also makes a special appearance.
title:私の男:
Director Kazuyoshi Kumakiri (*Sketches of Kaitan City*, *The End of Summer*) adapts the 138th Naoki Prize-winning novel by author Kazuki Sakuraba. Set against the majestic natural backdrop of Hokkaido, the film depicts an orphaned girl and the distant relative who takes her in as they live in close proximity, each harboring a profound inner emptiness. Starring Tadanobu Asano and Fumi Nikaido, the film features a stellar supporting cast including Kengo Kora and Tatsuya Fuji.

Hana, orphaned at the age of ten, is taken in by a distant relative named Jungo. Bound by their shared loneliness, the two live a quiet life in the rural town of Mombetsu, Hokkaido. However, their peace is shattered when news breaks of a murder committed on the drift ice. Upon hearing the report, the two flee the town as if pursued...
title:ヘブンズベル:
A suspense-action film from director Kyoji Kamui, who continues to define a hard-boiled worldview in works like "Moonlight Diner" and "7WAYS." Triggered by a family massacre 20 years ago, the story depicts the intersecting fates of those seeking the lost truth within a lawless zone known as "Area 4."

Natsumi was only ten years old when she survived the massacre two decades prior. Aside from her, the only other survivor was a "third man" whose existence was officially erased, while the two perpetrators met mysterious deaths. Twenty years later, the adult Natsumi receives a photograph from an anonymous sender. Guided by clues written on the back, she heads to the extraterritorial city of Area 4. Meanwhile, Rinka, a woman working for a dark organization, is tasked with collecting slush funds using hired convicts. However, she is betrayed by the organization and finds herself marked for death. Rescued by escaped prisoners Yabuki and Fujikawa, Rinka enters Area 4—the site of the transaction—to launder the stolen cash.

The film stars Chiaki Hiratsuka, a regular in Kamui’s films since "Smoky and Bitter." She is joined by fellow regular Miko Nakagawa and Kazunari Moriwaki (formerly of the duo "Saruganseki"). Furthermore, reflecting Director Kamui’s own history of appearing in special effects (tokusatsu) dramas, the cast features a lineup of genre veterans, including Shiro Izumi (Kyoryu Sentai Zyuranger), Yasuhiro Ishiwata (Choushinsei Flashman), Matsuri Kamizato (Ohsama Sentai King-Ohger), Eri Tanaka (Kamen Rider Kuuga), and suit actors Kazutoshi Yokoyama and Ryoko Gomi.
title:豹変と沈黙 日記でたどる沖縄戦への道: A documentary that seeks the truth of history through a meticulous reading of wartime diaries kept by former Japanese soldiers. Set primarily against the backdrop of the Second Sino-Japanese War, which escalated following the 1937 Marco Polo Bridge Incident, the film features readings from the diaries of three anonymous Japanese soldiers and one man from Okinawa who was eventually conscripted. By tracing the process through which ordinary Japanese citizens were drafted and molded into soldiers, the film unravels the little-known realities of the battlefield. The title’s "Sudden Change" refers to the erosion of humanity among Japanese soldiers on the battlefield, while "Silence" represents the post-war years during which these veterans remained tight-lipped, living with their wartime experiences locked away in their hearts. Directed by Yoshikazu Hara (*Song Before Dawn: Okinawa’s Hidden People with Disabilities*), with narration by voice actress Mai Aizawa.
title:名無しの子:
Director Ryo Takeuchi, an influencer in China known for documentaries like "The Yangtze River" that capture the nation's reality, turns his lens toward the Japanese orphans left behind in China.

1945, the end of World War II. Tens of thousands of young children living in former Manchuria were left behind, losing their nationality and becoming "nameless children." Yet, there were Chinese foster parents who, despite their own poverty, raised these orphans as their own children.

Traveling between Japan and China, this documentary features interviews with 100 people across three generations, including the orphans and their families. It portrays the harsh realities of those still at the mercy of fate 80 years later: the first generation who returned to Japan in the 1990s only to be driven to suicide attempts after failing to integrate into society; the second generation who, facing discrimination in both countries, formed the quasi-gang "Chinese Dragon" as a means of resistance; and the third generation who hide their mixed roots, unable to reveal their true identities even to their closest friends.
title:メロディがいた季節: A documentary capturing Mark Lester and Tracy Hyde—stars of the 1971 British coming-of-age masterpiece *Melody*—as they attend fan events in Japan and revisit filming locations near London. In 2022, the Yokohama movie theater Cinema Novecento held a screening event to commemorate the 50th anniversary of the release of *Melody*. In addition to special screenings of *Melody* and the film *Graduation Journey* (which Lester starred in in Japan) using new 35mm prints, fan events featuring Lester
title:ポール・マッカートニー：マン・オン・ザ・ラン:
For the one-day-only global theatrical release on February 19, it has been confirmed that an exclusive special conversation between Paul McCartney and director Morgan Neville will be shown as a theater-exclusive bonus! Experience this rare music documentary, featuring many of Paul McCartney and Wings' most iconic songs, on the big screen.

After the breakup of the Beatles, the most important rock band in history, what path did Paul McCartney choose?

*Paul McCartney: Man on the Run* is the latest documentary that traces Paul McCartney’s journey as he forged a new path by forming Wings with his wife Linda following the dissolution of the Beatles, told from an unprecedentedly personal and intimate perspective. Academy, Emmy, and Grammy Award-winning director Morgan Neville meticulously follows the trajectory of Paul’s creative rebirth—producing works that would define a new decade while facing numerous challenges and conflicts—to reveal sides of his story that have never been told before.

"December Rain" (23 min)

An inspired film based on the classic song "12-gatsu no Ame no Hi" (December Rain) from Happy End's self-titled debut album released in 1970. Marin, a girl searching for her birthplace, travels through her hometown in an attempt to recover paintings. An "inner-world road movie."

Director, Screenplay, Editor: Sawa Kawakami
Cast: Marin Hidaka, Jun Hosoi, Yuriko Tanaka, Ayu (Taro Monogatari), Kanon (Taro Monogatari), Yui Nishide, Konnichi Hakase (Nankyoku), Tomomi Nakai (Voice)
title:『ボロ家の春秋』: Gomi (Sada) begins living in a dilapidated mansion with a man he met by chance, but the man suddenly vanishes, and a string of eccentric characters starts showing up in his place. Based on a Naoki Prize-winning novel, this slapstick comedy features superb banter between Sada and a cast of quirky actors.
tmdb:640192: A moving drama based on the true story of a young woman who suddenly becomes paralyzed. Izumi delivers a powerful, soul-stirring performance as the heroine wandering the depths of despair, leaving viewers unable to hold back their tears.
tmdb:1568452: Akiko joins the Hinomaru University American football team as a manager to be near her crush, Yoshioka. However, under the direction of their advisor, Sawada, the team repeatedly resorts to dangerous plays during games, resulting in a suspension. To make matters worse, Captain Hirayama informs her that Yoshioka has died in an on-field accident, driving Akiko to despair. Spurred by his death, Akiko resolves to become a player herself and throws herself into grueling training. Meanwhile, Saori, a detective from the Josai Police Station, uncovers a grave secret involving the team and a former member named Sasaki.
title:CatVideoFest（キャットビデオフェスト）:
CatVideoFest is a globally beloved festival held annually since 2016, now screened in over 350 theaters worldwide, including the United States, Canada, and Europe. The festival features a carefully curated selection of the latest and greatest cat videos from across the globe. Cute, hilarious, and sometimes deeply moving—you can experience the full charm of these feline stars all at once on the big cinema screen!

As a theater-exclusive event, the festival has become a massive hit in the U.S., with attendance growing every year. Last year, it surpassed $1 million at the box office, proving that the unique experience of "watching cat videos together" continues to draw crowds to the cinema. These 73 minutes, filled with smiles and comfort, are truly a "dream come true for cat lovers."

Furthermore, the festival is built on a foundation of compassion: the act of watching the films directly supports cats in need. A portion of ticket proceeds is donated to animal welfare organizations. Since 2019, screenings around the world have raised over $150,000, saving the lives of countless cats. For the screenings in Japan, a portion of the proceeds will also be donated to local rescue groups to fund medical care, foster programs, and other essential support for cats in need.

Help cats just by watching. CatVideoFest is a celebration of feline wonder filled with kindness. Welcome to a "Kitty World" that fills the entire screen! This winter, treat yourself to the ultimate heartwarming cat video experience.
tmdb:1557893:
The second part of a two-part film project that serves as the culmination of the TV drama series "Kyojo" starring Takuya Kimura. While the first part, "Kyojo: Reunion," was released exclusively on Netflix, this sequel will be released in theaters. Set in "Kyojo," the academy for training future police officers, the film depicts instructor Kimichika Kazama (played by Kimura) confronting students burdened with various circumstances, as well as graduates who assemble to counter an ominous shadow closing in on Kazama.

The 205th class has been relentlessly put through the wringer by Kazama. As the darkness and secrets harbored by the students are exposed, some are forced to withdraw, yet Kazama’s scrutiny of the remaining students continues. Various dramas
tmdb:1576370: This documentary film was produced as the culmination of the anniversary year for Tohoshinki, the South Korean male vocal unit celebrating the 20th anniversary of their Japanese debut in 2025. Having debuted in Korea in 2004, Tohoshinki has continued to thrive on the global stage as representative artists of Asia. In Japan, they have enjoyed immense popularity since their debut in April 2005. Their "Tohoshinki 20th Anniversary LIVE TOUR ~ZONE~," held from November 2024 to April 2025 leading up to their milestone anniversary, drew approximately 350,000 fans across 22 performances in nine cities. Three of these shows were held at the Tokyo Dome, breaking their own record for the most performances at the venue by a foreign artist. This film captures Yunho and Changmin as they continue to venture into unprecedented territory, delving into the very heart of their identity.
title:シネマ歌舞伎 歌舞伎NEXT 朧の森に棲む鬼 幸四郎版:
"Kabuki NEXT: Oboro no Mori ni Sumu Oni" (Demons in the Oboro Forest), which was performed at the Shinbashi Enbujo in November and December 2024 and at the Hakataza in February 2025, comes to the big screen as part of the "Cinema Kabuki" series, allowing audiences to enjoy the spectacle of the Kabuki stage in movie theaters. Based on Shakespeare’s *Richard III*, the production is a Kabuki reimagining of Gekidan Shinkansen’s stage play of the same name, depicting a man at the mercy of his own lies and unbridled ambition. The stage production featured a double-cast for the protagonist Rai: Matsumoto Koshiro, who starred in the original Shinkansen version under his former name Ichikawa Somegoro, and Onoe Matsuya, who led the Shinkansen production *Metal Macbeth*. Cinema Kabuki will release both versions.

Driven by greed and a silver tongue, Rai swindles women and money until he enters the Oboro Forest—a place where those who set foot are said to turn into monsters—and strikes a forbidden pact with a demon. In exchange for his own lifeblood, he is promised the throne of the kingdom. As he leaves a trail of sacrifice in his wake, Rai steadily ascends toward the crown, but his rise to power also signals his ultimate destruction.

This "Koshiro Version" features Matsumoto Koshiro in the lead role of Rai. Supporting him is a distinguished cast including Bando Yajuro, Nakamura Tokizo, Bando Shingo, Onoe Ukon, Ichikawa Somegoro, and Onoe Matsuya. As with the original Shinkansen production, the script is by Kazuki Nakashima and the direction is by Hidenori Inoue.
title:幻愛 夢の向こうに:
STORY Is this love real, or an illusion?
Primary school teacher Lok (Li Chi-lok) manages to navigate his daily life while living with schizophrenia. One day, he falls in love at first sight with a woman named Yan Yan. The two are quickly drawn to one another, but Lok suffers in silence, unable to reveal the truth about his condition. As his symptoms worsen, their relationship begins to distort. Later, during a therapy session, Lok meets Ip Lam, a woman who looks exactly like Yan Yan. A graduate student majoring in psychology, Lam is researching "erotomania," a symptom of schizophrenia. Seeing Lok as the ideal subject for her research, she asks for his cooperation with her thesis. However, the two soon find themselves developing feelings that transcend the boundaries between researcher and patient—
title:ブーニン 天才ピアニストの沈黙と再生:
In 1985, at the age of 19, he won the International Chopin Piano Competition, igniting a whirlwind of fervor with his breathtaking performance. Then, in 2013, he suddenly vanished from the spotlight.

Beset by illness, injury, paralysis of the left hand, and major surgery—trials that threatened his very life as a pianist—he has overcome years of suffering and inner conflict to return to the stage once more. This music documentary captures his latest performance at Suntory Hall in December 2025 in its entirety, delving deep into Stanislav Bunin’s inner world while offering a transcendent musical experience.

Interweaving the bond with his wife, Eiko, who walked the path to recovery alongside him, and testimonies from renowned pianists who hold him in the highest esteem, the production team—who followed his journey of rebirth with intimate access—paints a portrait of the vista this genius has finally reached after his long struggle. Featuring a selection of masterpieces handpicked by Bunin himself, we invite you to experience his exquisite artistry on the big screen as he pursues his simple wish: "I want to give beautiful performances that move people."

Featuring a complete recording of his latest performance at Suntory Hall in December 2025, this music documentary offers a supreme musical experience while delving deep into the inner world of Stanislav Bunin. Interweaving the bond with his wife, Eiko, who walked the path to his comeback by his side, and testimonies from renowned pianists who hold him in the highest esteem, the production team—who followed his journey of rebirth through intimate, long-term coverage—depicts the vista the genius pianist finally reached after a period of suffering and struggle. Including a selection of masterpieces handpicked by Bunin himself, the film invites you to experience his exquisite artistry on the big screen as he shares his heartfelt wish: "I want to give beautiful performances that move people."
title:Eno: Brian Eno is a legendary artist who has continuously embodied the very concept of "innovation" in music and art. As a musician, producer, visual artist, and activist, he has remained ahead of his time in every field—a truly unparalleled presence who has presented a clear vision for over 50 years. Now, *Eno*, the world’s first fully generative documentary that delves into the essence of the man himself, arrives in Japan. Directed by Gary Hustwit, the film combines extensive interviews with Brian Eno and over 500 hours of rare archival footage, utilizing "Brain One" (an anagram of Brian Eno), an automated generation system co-developed with artist Brendan Dawes. This groundbreaking work defies cinematic conventions to create a completely new experience where the structure and content change with every screening. Just like the ever-evolving Eno, do not miss this once-in-a-lifetime viewing experience!
title:幻愛 夢の向こうに:
Terence Lau (*Twilight of the Warriors: Walled In*) and Cecilia Choi star in this poignant love story depicting a man and woman wandering the boundary between reality and illusion.

Primary school teacher Lai Chi-lok manages to lead a functional life despite suffering from schizophrenia. He falls in love at first sight with a woman named Yan Yan, but as they grow closer, he is unable to confess his illness to her. As his symptoms worsen, it is eventually revealed that Yan Yan herself was a hallucination. During a therapy session, Lok meets Ip Lam, a woman who is the spitting image of Yan Yan. A graduate student majoring in psychology, Lam is researching "erotomania," a symptom of schizophrenia. Though she initially asks Lok to cooperate with her thesis as an ideal research subject, the two soon develop feelings that transcend the roles of researcher and patient.

The film is directed by Kiwi Chow (*Ten Years*, *Revolution of Our Times*). Terence Lau plays Chi-lok, a young man seeking love while struggling with schizophrenia, and Cecilia Choi plays both Yan Yan and Lam. Their performances won Best Actor and Best Actress, respectively, at the 26th Hong Kong Film Critics Society Awards.
title:ゴーレム: One of four dark sci-fi works by Piotr Szulkin, a pioneer of Polish dystopian cinema, produced while navigating the strict censorship of the socialist regime during the late 1970s and 1980s. The film depicts the harsh reality of clones created for human evolution through a style that represents the pinnacle of sharp satire and surrealism. A man arrested on suspicion of murder is interrogated by the police, but he cannot recall the details of the crime or even his own life. He is eventually returned to a world of madmen, deranged dentists, murderous doctors, and scientists who believe the secret of human creation lies within the walls of a cast-iron oven. The man’s journey to discover who he is and what it means to be human intersects with them all. The film will receive its Japanese theatrical premiere in 2026 as part of the special retrospective "Polish Dark Sci-Fi: The Apocalypse Tetralogy" (opening February 21, 2026, at Theater Image Forum).
title:どうしようもない10人: Set in a future where Japan has been partitioned into three territories by the United States, China, and Russia, this gun-action spectacle follows ten individuals driven by greed through a web of betrayal and deception, culminating in intense dual-pistol shootouts. Following a revolution where the citizenry slaughtered every politician, Japan has been left in ruins. Yahei, who has come into possession of a miraculous bulb known as "Manzu no Moto," amasses a fortune by selling "Manzu"—a single grain of which can satisfy hunger for an entire day. However, his wealth is stolen by a man wearing a Tengu mask. Realizing the thief is actually Dragon, the man who abandoned his family, Yahei hires an assassin named Ageha to do the job. But after taking down Dragon, Ageha vanishes and cuts off all contact. As a cycle of violence and greed continues among various assassins, the whereabouts of the money remain a mystery. It is then that Yahei receives a mysterious invitation. At the designated location, the assassins lie in wait, and a final battle to the death among the ten gathered souls begins. The film stars Shigefumi Fukuzawa, known for his roles in *Ohsama Sentai King-Ohger* and the NHK Taiga drama *Berabou*. It marks the directorial debut of Hiroyasu Kitagawa, who served as chief assistant director on major productions such as *Bayside Shakedown THE FINAL* and *The Wolverine*.
title:ショート・パルス 5つの鼓動: This anthology brings together five short films by world-renowned directors at the forefront of contemporary cinema. The collection features: *The Realist* (2025 / Japan-China / 11 min), directed by Neo Sora (*Happyend*) and based on illustrator Momoe Narazaki’s short story "I Will Go Ahead," which won the Pardino d'oro for Best International Short
tmdb:1219388: This documentary follows the struggles of four high school students in Shimane Prefecture and their teacher as they experience theater for the first time. At the Kakeya Branch of Mitoya High School—the smallest prefectural high school in Shimane with just 70 students—four teenagers begin their journey into drama. Each a novice to the stage, they grapple with their own insecurities—from social anxiety to a lack of academic focus or passion—as they gradually devote themselves to the craft. Under the guidance of their advisor, Yoshihiro Kameo, they select Osamu Dazai’s classic "Run, Melos!" and head to the regional competition with high hopes. However, they are soon overwhelmed by the high level of the main Mitoya High School campus, and the COVID-19 pandemic results in a competition held behind closed doors. Though they fail to advance to the prefectural level and fear their efforts will go unseen, their youth takes an unexpected turn. The film has earned high praise at various festivals, winning four awards including the Special Jury Prize and Audience Award at the 14th Shimokitazawa Film Festival, and was an official selection at the Tokyo Documentary Film Festival 2022.
title:メロスたち: This is the sequel to the 2022 documentary "Run! Run Run Melos," which followed four high school students in Shimane Prefecture as they discovered the joy of performing on stage. The drama club at the Kakeya Branch of Shimane Prefectural Mitoya High School had earned unexpectedly high praise for their original play "Run! Run Run Melos," based on Osamu Dazai’s classic novel "Run, Melos!" Since then, their advisor Mr. Yoshihiro Kameo, who first guided them into the world of theater, has transferred to another school, and the four members have moved into their final year of high school. As they each begin to decide on their future paths, Shogo Sota finds himself standing alone on stage at the Chugoku regional drama competition. The film captures the individual choices of the four students as they face graduation, grappling with loneliness, conflict, and anxiety. It also features footage of their March 2023 reunion just before graduation, where they performed a show titled "Graduation Ceremony" before large audiences in Shimokitazawa, Tokyo, and Matsue, Shimane.
tmdb:1536538: A documentary tracing the final three and a half years of world-renowned musician Ryuichi Sakamoto, who passed away in March 2023. Based on the 2024 NHK documentary "Last Days: Ryuichi Sakamoto's Final Days," which garnered a significant public response, this film version incorporates new elements such as unfinished music and footage. Throughout his career, Sakamoto was a multifaceted artist who transcended boundaries between music, art, film, and literature. Centered on his personal diaries—where he meticulously recorded the sights and sounds of his life—and featuring rare private footage and portraits provided with the full cooperation of his family, the film reflects on his battle with cancer and the creative activities he pursued until the very end. Through casual daily musings, his struggles with mortality, and profound words on the nature of music, the film explores how this peerless musician faced the end of his life and what he sought to leave behind. It captures Sakamoto’s final days and his unwavering passion for creating the ideal sound he sought throughout his life, set against the beautiful natural sounds and landscapes that captivated him in his later years. Dancer and actor Min Tanaka, a close friend of Sakamoto, performs the readings of the diary entries.
title:工場 鉄西区 第一部: Part one of the documentary trilogy "Tie Xi Qu: West of the Tracks," a nine-hour epic by director Wang Bing ("The Ditch") that chronicles the decline of the Tiexi District, a heavy industrial zone in Shenyang, China. Originally established in Mukden, Manchuria, during the Japanese occupation, the district grew into a vital industrial hub of 20th-century China before its prosperity eventually faded with the changing times. The film takes its camera into three factories, capturing workers who once braved hazards like accidents and lead poisoning as they now idle in facilities slated for closure, as well as state-owned factory managers struggling with the pressures of privatization and failing operations. The trilogy won the Grand Prize at the 2003 Yamagata International Documentary Film Festival and received its first Japanese theatrical release in 2013 to coincide with the premiere of Wang Bing’s "Three Sisters."
tmdb:848950:
The fifth feature film from Patricia Mazuy—editor of Agnès Varda’s *Vagabond* and director of films such as *Paul Sanchez Is Back!*—this neo-noir exposes the evil lurking within modern society through a series of murders surrounding half-brothers who inherit a bowling alley.

Armand wanders the streets, living out of strangers' cars parked outside "Le Cargo," the club where he occasionally works. One day, his half-brother Guillaume, a police officer, appears to inform him of the death of their father, a hunter. Seeking to reconcile with his estranged brother, Guillaume offers Armand the chance to manage "Bowling Saturne," the bowling alley their father left behind. However, after taking over, Armand begins causing friction, even demanding that his father’s old associates stay away. Meanwhile, the hunters are provoked by animal rights activist Xuan in the media and nearly erupt into violence before Guillaume intervenes. As Guillaume falls in love with Xuan after a chance encounter, the bodies of several young women are discovered in a local cemetery, revealing that a
title:FRIDAY: A music documentary chronicling a legendary live house with a 40-year history in Chojamachi, Naka-ku, Yokohama. Tucked away on the third floor of a multi-tenant building, "FRIDAY" is a venue with a capacity of only 50 seats. It is widely known as the home base for Crazy Ken Band, who have performed there since their days of obscurity and continue to hold monthly shows there even after their major breakthrough. Managed by its 80-year-old proprietor, Junichi Isohara, the club has long been beloved by a roster of illustrious musicians, including George Yanagi, Ryudo Uzaki, Crystal King, and Haruo Chikada. Centered on interviews and live footage of artists who adore the venue and its master—such as Ken Yokoyama, Show, Hiroaki Yamazaki, Monsieur Yoshizaki, Haruo Chikada, Ryudo Uzaki, and Aya Nakano—this film explores the enduring allure of FRIDAY and Junichi Isohara. The documentary is directed by Shikai Kyodai, whose previous works include "Ima o Ureu Kimi mo Haru ni Nareba" and "Boku to, Kare to."
title:ロビー！ 4000億円を懸けた仁義なき18ホール: Popular Korean actor Ha Jung-woo stars in and directs this comedy-drama depicting a young CEO’s struggle as he tackles his first-ever round of business golf to secure a massive national project. Chang-wook, the head of a small tech startup, begins lobbying efforts to save his company from the brink of bankruptcy. Although a brilliant engineer, he is clueless about the unwritten rules of the business world and a poor salesman. To get a piece of a 4-trillion-won national project, he finds himself playing a high-stakes game of golf with Director Choi, the man who holds the power over the bidding process. Chang-wook arrives fully prepared, even inviting Se-bin, a young pro golfer and Director Choi’s personal favorite. However, the day descends into total chaos when a rival CEO and the Minister—who happens to be both Choi’s boss and his wife—unexpectedly show up. The film features an ensemble cast including Kim Eui-sung (Train to Busan), Park Byung-eun (In Our Prime), Lee Dong-hwi (Extreme Job), Kang Mal-geum (Lucky Chan-sil), Choi Si-won of the boy band SUPER JUNIOR, and Kang Hae-lim (Netflix’s Somebody). Kim Kyung-chan (1987: When the Day Comes) co-wrote the screenplay alongside Ha Jung-woo.
title:じっちゃ！:
Set against the seasonal landscapes of Tsugaru City, Aomori Prefecture, this human comedy depicts the bond between a granddaughter who moves from Tokyo and her grandfather, a local melon farmer. Yuki Mikami relocates to Tsugaru City to live with her grandfather, Taisuke, through the Local Revitalization Cooperator program. Assigned to the Tourism and Brand Strategy Division at the city hall, she struggles with her unfamiliar duties to promote the city’s charms nationwide, yet finds solace in her daily life with her grandfather. After a whirlwind year, a colleague she has feelings for approaches her with a business opportunity in Tokyo. Just then, Taisuke, who has rarely spoken of his past, reveals a secret he has kept for 40 years—a story of a hidden bond that kept him in Aomori all those years.

Shizuka Nakamura (*Tida*) stars as the granddaughter, Yuki, alongside veteran actor Takehiko Ono as Taisuke. The supporting cast includes Kai Ogasawara of the dance and vocal group "BULLET TRAIN," Takeshi Nadagi, Harumi Shuhama, and Kanji Tsuda. Directed and written by Toshimitsu Chimura (*100-Second Fist King*, *Keno-shiru*), the film highlights the scenery and landmarks of Tsugaru City. It was produced to commemorate the 20th anniversary of the incorporation of Tsugaru City.
title:もしも脳梗塞になったなら: A socially conscious drama directed by Takafumi Ota (known for *Sunflower Hill 1983 Summer* and *The House of the Rising Sun*), based on his own personal battle with a stroke. Film director Ryutaro Otaki, who lives alone, suddenly suffers a cerebral infarction one day. His heart function drops to 20%, he loses half his vision, and he finds himself unable to speak properly. Even when he calls friends, they don't believe him, and when he posts about his condition on social media, he is met only with irrelevant advice and online harassment. Just as he is driven to the brink of dying alone, helping hands are extended to him from unexpected sources. The protagonist, Ryutaro Otaki, is played by Shunsuke Kubozuka, who starred in *Hanagatami* by director Nobuhiko Obayashi, under whom Ota studied. The cast also features Takemi Fujii as the sister, Misato Tanaka as the mother, and Tomoko Fujita and Shiro Sano as the friends who support Otaki online.
title:DEAD OR ZOMBIE:
Set in a city overrun by zombies, this unconventional drama depicts the quiet survival of individuals struggling with the burdens of life. Originally released as the 2022 short film *DEAD OR ZOMBIE: Even if Zombies Appear, We Won't Change Our Self-Esteem*, the project was expanded with prequels and sequels to form a single feature-length film consisting of four episodes.

A zombification phenomenon breaks out in a regional Japanese city. Saki, a high school girl who had been a shut-in in her room, remains in the quarantine zone to care for her zombified family, carving out a life of survival (Episode 1: "Even if Zombies Appear, We Won't Change Our Self-Esteem").

An ancient virus is discovered in samples collected from an asteroid by a Japanese unmanned space probe, and analysis begins at the National Space Research Institute... (Episode 0: "The Origin of Life").

With the Japanese government’s response lagging, the U.S. Forces Japan take control of the quarantine zone. Meanwhile, China sends in a research institution to investigate the zombies (Episode 2: "The Will of the Universe").

Saki, who once lived with her zombified family in the quarantine zone, learns that the Chinese research institution is collecting the remains of the infected. She infiltrates the zone to discover what happened to her family... (Episode 3: "The Meaning of
tmdb:1543797:
A mystery period drama based on the award-winning novel by Sayako Nagai—recipient of both the Naoki Prize and the Yamamoto Shugoro Prize—marking the first screen collaboration between Tasuku Emoto and Ken Watanabe.

Set in the Edo period, the story begins on a snowy night near the Morita-za theater in Kobikicho, where a beautiful youth named Kikunosuke successfully carries out a revenge killing to avenge his father. Witnessed by many, the event is hailed as a noble tale of justice. A year and a half later, a country samurai named Soichiro Kase, claiming to be a relative of Kikunosuke, arrives at the Morita-za seeking the full story of the incident. As he interviews those connected to Kikunosuke, the facts slowly unravel, eventually exposing a "secret" hidden beneath the surface of the revenge.

Tasuku Emoto stars as Soichiro Kase, the samurai pursuing the truth, while Ken Watanabe delivers a commanding performance as Kinji Shinoda, the head playwright at Morita-za who orchestrates the schemes within the theater. The ensemble cast features Kento Nagao (Naniwa Danshi) as Kikunosuke Ino, the young man who achieved his revenge; Kazuki Kitamura as Sakubee, the outlaw who killed Kikunosuke’s father; Koji Seto as Ippachi, a theater barker; Kenichi Takito as Yosaburo Sagara, the fight choreographer; Kazuya Takahashi as Hotaru Yoshizawa, an *onnagata* actor and costume manager; and Bokuzo Masana as Kyuzo, the prop master. The film is written and directed by Takashi Minamoto, known for the period drama *Chushingura Rhapsody No. 5: Nakamura Nakazo’s Rise to Fame* and the film *Until the Lights Come Back*.
tmdb:1542261:
The 45th feature film in the beloved national anime series "Doraemon." This film is a remake of the fourth entry in the series, "Nobita and the Castle of the Undersea Devil," originally released in 1983.

During summer vacation, Nobita and his friends find themselves divided over where to go camping. At Doraemon's suggestion, they decide to set up camp in the middle of the ocean. Using secret gadgets like the "Underwater Buggy" and the "Adaptation Ray," they enjoy their time on the seabed. However, after discovering a sunken ship, they encounter a mysterious young man named El, who is revealed to be an undersea dweller from the "Mu Federation" that spans the ocean floor. The undersea people, who harbor a deep distrust of surface dwellers, are hesitant to trust Nobita and his friends. Meanwhile, news arrives that the "Castle of the Undersea Devil"—a place feared by all undersea people—has begun to move.

The film is directed by Tetsuo Yajima, known for his work on storyboards and direction for the "Doraemon" TV series and films, as well as "Pokémon the Movie: Secrets of the Jungle." Actress Airi Taira and the comedy duo "Alco & Peace" serve as promotional ambassadors and make guest voice appearances in the film. This installment also marks the first time a film in the series will be screened in 4D.
title:ENHYPEN [WALK THE LINE SUMMER EDIT:
A concert film capturing the first-ever stadium performance in Japan by the seven-member global boy group from South Korea, ENHYPEN.

As part of ENHYPEN's world tour in Japan, the dome tour "ENHYPEN WORLD TOUR ‘WALK THE LINE’ IN JAPAN" was held from November 2024 to January 2025. As an encore to that tour, "ENHYPEN WORLD TOUR ‘WALK THE LINE’ IN JAPAN SUMMER EDITION" took place on July 5 and 6, 2025, at Tokyo's Ajinomoto Stadium, and on August 2 and 3 at Osaka's Yanmar Stadium Nagai.

This film captures the full performance from the July 6 show at Ajinomoto Stadium. In addition to the passionate performances delivered on the group's largest-ever stage, the film brings to the screen behind-the-scenes rehearsals, candid moments from their daily lives while traveling, and the overwhelming energy of ENGENE (ENHYPEN's fandom) that filled the stadium with excitement.

The film will be screened in a variety of formats, including standard 2D, the three-sided multi-projection ScreenX, the immersive 4DX, and ULTRA 4DX, which combines both ScreenX and 4DX.
title:TRAVERSE 2 Next Level: The sequel to the action film "TRAVERSE," starring martial arts karateka Jun Tabei. Five years have passed since the battle against the Japanese branch of the massive crime syndicate "Sasori" (Scorpion). To escape the syndicate's reach, Jun Takanashi and his stepdaughter Rina have been living quietly in a remote mountain area, concealing their names and identities. However, Sasori dispatches the "Cleaners"—an elite unit of former soldiers—to track them down. Eventually, the Cleaners locate the father and daughter and launch an assault. Following the previous film, Jun Tabei, who brings his experience as an action actor to the role, returns to star as the protagonist Jun Takanashi. Newcomer Saki Nakano was selected through auditions to play his stepdaughter, Rina. Zen-tetsu Haku, who served as the action director for the first film and is an active stuntman, takes the helm as director.
title:フレデリック・ワイズマン監督 緊急追悼上映: The monumental work of documentary master Wiseman, who passed away on February 16, 2026, now.
title:夜鶯 ―ある洋館での殺人事件―:
That night, the case begins to move once more—

A group of film industry insiders, each harboring their own secrets, are summoned to a mysterious mansion. Their mission: adapt a gruesome, real-life murder case into a movie. But there is a twist—the "real killer" is right there in the room! An unpredictable masterpiece of mystery entertainment has finally arrived.

Shanghai, 1940s—a city in the throes of post-war chaos. A washed-up director, a struggling screenwriter, a former starlet seeking a comeback, and a shrewd producer. Hungry for fame and fortune, these industry figures gather at a luxurious estate at the invitation of a mysterious tycoon. Their task is to craft a blockbuster screenplay in a single night, based on an unsolved, sensationalized murder case. However, this top-secret meeting includes a guest who knows the truth better than anyone: the actual murderer.

Driven by both terror and ambition, the group engages in a life-or-death battle of wits to extract the truth from the killer and weave it into their script. In a locked room where fact and fiction blur, the truth they uncover begins to intertwine with their own dark pasts and dangerous secrets. Will they complete a cinematic masterpiece for the ages, or the final chapter of their own destinies?
title:コーナー・オブ・ジ・アース カムチャツカ:
The journey began on February 24, 2022, at Abu Dhabi Airport. Australian filmmakers Spencer Frost and Guy Williment, along with surfers Fraser Dovell and Letty Mortenson, planned a trip to Kamchatka in the Russian Far East in search of new waves along its frozen coastline. This adventure documentary chronicles the friendship and journey of these young men as they take on the overwhelming wilderness of Russia's remote Kamchatka Peninsula.

Bordered by the Pacific Ocean and the Sea of Okhotsk and home to over 150 volcanoes, Kamchatka is a true hidden frontier. It is said that until around 1991, access was prohibited not only for foreigners but even for Russian citizens. Because it remains largely unexplored, it is considered one of the most precious places on Earth, where vast, untouched nature still thrives.

With little local information available, the group relied on a surfer living in Kamchatka. After two years of preparation via smartphone, their visas were finally issued, and they were ready to depart. However, just one hour before boarding their flight to Moscow at a transit airport, unbelievable news flashed across the television: Russia had invaded Ukraine. Amidst the chaos, they had no choice but to continue their journey to Kamchatka, where their friend was waiting.

Upon setting foot in the region, they were met with Mi-8 helicopters, snowmobiles, and campsites buried in snow. There, they finally met their Kamchatkan friend for the first time. This is a heartwarming adventure documentary that reaffirms the power of friendship to connect people across different nations and backgrounds.
title:ゴリラホール:
Set at the real-life music venue "GORILLA HALL OSAKA," this youth ensemble drama depicts the struggles and growth of young people living for music. Asako works at Gorilla Hall while pursuing her own musical dreams, but her band "GIRL TALKING ABOUT LOVE," for which she is the guitarist and vocalist, is struggling against the harshness of reality. Meanwhile, her boyfriend Ichiya is finding success as the frontman of the band "SUMMER JOE," steadily climbing the ladder to stardom. When a major label announces an audition, both Ichiya’s band and Asako’s group are given a chance to compete. Despite her doubts, Asako takes a new step forward with her bandmates, only to be confronted by an unexpected reality on that very stage.

Kansai-based singer-songwriter AIK makes her acting debut in her first starring role as Asako, with Wataru Momma ("Manji Reverse") playing her boyfriend, Ichiya. The supporting cast features Arata Furuta, Ayumi Ito, Tomomitsu Yamaguchi, and Tomoka Kurotani. Koji Uehara ("Before the Rainbow Falls") wrote and directed the film, with music by Kj (Dragon Ash, The Ravens) and actor Kyosuke Yabe serving as executive advisor.
title:Playback: A drama captured in beautiful monochrome cinematography, following an actor at a crossroads in life as he moves between the past and present to take a new step forward. The film is the second feature from up-and-coming director Sho Miyake. Haji, an actor on the verge of turning 40, finds himself at a professional standstill, burdened by a mounting series of personal struggles including failing health and a separation from his wife. It is then that Bon, a high school friend he hasn't seen in years, appears and invites him to their hometown for the wedding of another old friend, Yuji. While traveling to his hometown in Bon’s car, Haji drifts off to sleep, only to wake up and find himself transported back to his high school days twenty years ago—yet still in his adult body. Led by Jun Murakami, the film features a talented ensemble cast including Kiyohiko Shibukawa, Masaki Miura, Makiko Watanabe, and Shun Sugata.
title:行方不明: A featured film of the Japan-Philippines Film Festival in Tokyo (February 28 – March 6, 2026, at K's cinema).
tmdb:912480: A Canadian psychological thriller depicting the madness of a woman captivated by a brutal serial killer, this film won Best Screenplay, Best Feature, and Best Score at the 2023 Fantasia International Film Festival. Ludovic Chevalier is a serial killer arrested on suspicion of kidnapping, imprisoning, and torturing young girls, filming their deaths to be streamed on the deep web (known as "RED ROOMS"). For Kelly-Anne, a successful fashion model, attending the trial of the man whose heinous crimes have shocked the world has become a quiet daily routine. Starring as the protagonist obsessed with the gruesome case is Juliette Gariépy, who balances a modeling career with filmmaking. The film is written and directed by Pascal Plante, whose 2020 film *Nadia, Butterfly* was an Official Selection at the 73rd Cannes Film Festival.
title:月あかりの下で ある定時制高校の記憶: Directed by Naoko Ota, a former part-time high school teacher, this documentary captures the lives of various students attending a nighttime high school. Set at Saitama Prefectural Urawa Commercial High School, the film follows a single class over four years, from their enrollment in 2002 to their graduation in 2006. From students in flashy attire who lash out at teachers to those unable to attend school due to domestic violence or those struggling with self-harm, the film portrays young people who support one another while grappling with deep-seated issues in their families and social lives.
title:まなぶ 通信制中学 60年の空白を越えて: This documentary, filmed over five years, captures the lives of elderly students attending a correspondence junior high school after being unable to receive compulsory education due to various circumstances during the post-war chaos. These correspondence courses were established for those who could not complete their three years of middle school during the turmoil of the war and its aftermath. At Kanda Hitotsubashi Junior High School in Chiyoda—one of the few remaining institutions of its kind—elderly students arrive to attend their lessons. Twice a month on their days off, they receive instruction from teachers the age of their children or grandchildren. Though bewildered by school life after a 60-year hiatus, their expressions overflow with the joy of learning as they return to their youth. Overcoming the hardships of caregiving and personal illness, they share their family stories and laugh together during breaks. Through their school life, the film explores why people still strive to learn even as they approach the final chapters of their lives. Directed by Naoko Ota, director of "Under the Moonlight: Memories of a Night High School."

Meanwhile, Masaki Kosaka, a production director for the tournament's broadcast, meets Masato during his research and discovers that he is a childhood friend of both Park and Sara. tmdb:1013701:
A documentary capturing the present state of the "Beautiful Island" of Taiwan, documenting the research process of the film collective Kuzoku—acclaimed for works such as *Saudade*, *Bangkok Nites*, and *TENZO*—as they prepare for their upcoming feature. Known for their method of conducting exhaustive location research before filming, Kuzoku plans to shoot their next project in Taiwan in 2026. Since 2020, they have made repeated visits to the island, continuing their fieldwork even amidst the pandemic. 

Guided by the music echoing through the streets, they journey to the villages of Taiwan’s indigenous peoples: the Amis settlement of Tafalong in Hualien County; the Seediq villages reached by crossing the 3,000-meter peaks of the Central Mountain Range; and the Paiwan villages at the island’s southernmost tip. As they connect with the people they meet—youth rapping in endangered indigenous tongues and artists updating traditional melodies into contemporary sounds—they also confront a history of invasion and colonization by various nations, including Japan.
title:映画 潜行一千里: A making-of documentary following the production of "Bangkok Nites," directed by Katsuya Tomita of the film collective "Kuzoku" and released in theaters in February 2017. Ten years in the making, "Bangkok Nites" was filmed entirely on location, traversing 4,000 kilometers across Thailand and Laos. From Thaniya Road—Bangkok’s red-light district catering to Japanese clientele—to the northeastern Isan region, the border town of Nong Khai on the banks of the Mekong River, and deep into the remote reaches of Laos, the production was a continuous journey where the act of filming became the journey itself. The documentary captures Kuzoku’s signature production style: a process where locals are cast as actors and join the crew as collaborators to bring a film to life. The documentary is directed by Masahiro Mukoyama of "Studio Ishi" (the visual unit of the hip-hop crew "stillichimiya"), who also served as the cinematographer for the original film.
title:原発をとめた裁判長 そして原発をとめる農家たち: This documentary follows a former chief judge who campaigns to warn of the dangers of nuclear power, and the people of Fukushima who are reviving the agriculture they once abandoned due to radiation through solar power. In 2014, Chief Judge Hideaki Higuchi of the Fukui District Court issued a landmark ruling ordering the suspension of operations at the Ohi Nuclear Power Plant. Following his retirement, he began a mission to explain the structural vulnerabilities common to all nuclear plants in Japan. Hiroyuki Kawai, a leading lawyer in nuclear litigation, launches a new legal battle based on the "Higuchi Theory," which argues that nuclear plants are structurally incapable of withstanding the country's frequent earthquakes. Meanwhile, in Fukushima, farmer Megumi Kondo, who was forced out of business by radioactive contamination, finds a path to agricultural revival through "solar sharing"—generating electricity above active farmland. With the help of environmental scholar Tetsuya Iida, she launches one of Japan’s largest agrivoltaic projects. The film is directed by Hiroyasu Obara, director of *Voices of the Abandoned*.

title:WITH: On May 16, 2022, filming for director Yoshimasa Ishibashi’s "Six Singing Women"—a film centered on the theme of "symbiosis with nature"—began, primarily in Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, a place where primeval forests still endure. As the COVID-19 pandemic began to subside, production moved forward with the support of local residents who placed great hope in the project. These expectations stirred the hearts of city officials striving to transform the town and residents seeking to revitalize their community through cinema. However, the production was beset by unforeseen challenges, including the cancellation of planned schedules and various troubles on set. Amidst these struggles, the true sentiments they wished to convey and the dreams they hoped to realize began to surface. What did this film ultimately create? This is a record of a year and a half spent following those living in harmony with nature, people, the town, and the era, all through the journey of a single motion picture.
title:絶倫!! 好色一代［『ニッポンの猥褻』改題版］＊: Known by its theatrical title, *Obscenity in Japan*, this film was produced to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Shintoho Eiga. Tetsu Hashimoto (Kubo), a self-proclaimed sexologist entering his later years, reflects on his life through the lens of his vivid sexual experiences. Shinji Kubo, hailed as the "Emperor of Porno" for his commanding presence in *pink* cinema, captivated audiences with a surging energy epitomized by his role as Ozaki in the *Widow's Boarding House* series (1974–1984).
tmdb:366331:
<Director’s Statement>

I felt a desire to record this event. I am a historian and a sociologist. I believed that what I must do now is to document this and preserve it for future generations.

I had never made a film before. I had never even been interested in filmmaking. However, gathering fragments of historical records and weaving them into a single world is exactly what I have done in my previous writings. Whether the medium I handled was text or video was of no consequence in this instance.

It goes without saying that this is not a work I created alone. The people who filmed on-site at the time and those who sat for interviews all cooperated without compensation.

Above all, the protagonists of this film are every single person who appears in it. they are diverse in gender, generation, social status, nationality, origin, and ideology. I found the sight of such varied people gathering in a single place to be both a rare occurrence and something profoundly powerful and beautiful.

Such miraculous moments rarely visit a nation or a society. As a historian, I know this to be true. What I have done—what I set out to do—is simply to record such a moment, and nothing more.

I believe this is a film that can be interpreted in many ways. After viewing it, I hope you will share your honest thoughts with the person sitting next to you. It is the audience, and society as a collective, that gives meaning to a film. From that dialogue, I believe something new will be born, both for you and for society.

Fifteen years after the Great East Japan Earthquake. A Self-Defense Force member who lost his wife and child, and a boy who lost his parents. This is a story of rebirth for two individuals as they begin to walk forward alongside their experiences of loss.

Director Yuji Kakizaki, who earned high acclaim for his previous work *The Sun Falls*—a profound human drama focusing on the resolve of a samurai’s wife and the bond of a married couple—now takes on a new challenge: a story of "loss" and "rebirth."

The film stars Yasuyuki Maekawa, who left an unforgettable impression in the director's previous work. He breathes life into Third Lieutenant Kasuga, a JSDF member who continues his duties despite losing his wife and young daughter in the tsunami, through a performance imbued with quiet strength. Set in the disaster-stricken areas of the Great East Japan Earthquake, Kasuga spends his days providing support at evacuation centers, facing people head-on while carrying his own deep pain. title:平田雄己監督特集上映Lost in Images: One day, Ayano goes on a picnic with her boyfriend, Shuichi, and his daughter, Minato. However, the atmosphere between the three is slightly strained. Eventually, they drift apart. Minato immerses herself in memories of her late mother, Miho. Shuichi sees visions of his past family. And then, Miho appears before Ayano.
title:361 – White and Black –: Masato Kamijo (Ryota Nagano), living in a port town, happens to see a press conference on television where the world Go champion, Park Han-myeong (Park Yu-chun), declares, "I have a rival in Japan." While everyone assumes the rival is Sara Yonehara, 7th dan (Nao Hoshino), anticipation grows as Park enters a tournament in Japan with a total prize pool of 100 million yen, sparking hope for a direct showdown with this mysterious rival. Meanwhile, Masaki Kosaka (Koudai Matsuoka), a director for the tournament’s television coverage, meets Masato during his research and discovers that Masato is a childhood friend of both Park and Sara. Upon learning that Masato is an online Go champion, Masaki encourages him to enter the tournament, only to find that Masato is traumatized by a past event that makes him unable to play Go face-to-face. Through Masaki’s unconventional plan, Masato eventually enters the competition and faces off against formidable opponents. What was the tragic event that occurred between Masato, Park, and Sara in the past? What is the secret hidden within the tournament? Through his matches, Masato arrives at a single truth...
title:めぐる／エイン: On the morning of a job interview, college student Megumi wakes from a nightmare. It is a recurring memory of the death of her younger sister, Nao, who had been tormented by bullying. Having overslept, Megumi rushes to the station, bumping into a man in a suit as she slips onto a train just before departure. That split-second delay alters the fate of Keiichi, a middle-aged man on his way to the hospital. Meanwhile, on the station platform, Yuki, a high school student harboring deep emotional scars, and Meguru, a young man studying for his university entrance exams, share a chance encounter. One person’s step ripples outward, touching the lives and futures of others. As their lives intersect, diverge, and occasionally collide, they ultimately find a small, fragile glimmer of hope.
title:エリス＆トム ―ボサノヴァ名盤誕生秘話―: In February 1974, Elis Regina, one of Brazil’s greatest singers, and Antônio Carlos Jobim (Tom Jobim), who achieved worldwide fame in the 1960s with the massive hit "The Girl from Ipanema," gathered in Los Angeles to collaborate on the album *Elis & Tom*. Even now, half a century later, it remains a Bossa Nova masterpiece that shines brilliantly in the music history of not only Brazil but the world. However, behind the scenes of the recording, various problems and frictions were brewing. The chemistry between Elis, the pop star, and Tom, the founder of Bossa Nova who preferred the simple music of the good old days, was disastrous. Furthermore, a rift formed between Tom and César Camargo Mariano, Elis's husband at the time and the album's arranger. What exactly happened during the recording of their album? This must-see documentary delves into those secrets.
title:RIP SLYME THE MOVIE 25th ANNIVERSA:
A live performance and documentary film featuring RIP SLYME, the pioneering group credited with pushing Japanese hip-hop into the mainstream. Following their major debut in 2001, the group made history with their 2002 sophomore album *TOKYO CLASSIC*, which became the first hip-hop album in Japan to achieve one million copies in sales. Producing a string of hits such as "One," "Rakuen Baby," and "Nettaiya," they paved the way for a generation of artists who followed in their footsteps. In April 2025, the five original members reunited for a limited time leading up to March 22, 2026, the 25th anniversary of their major debut. The release of their new song "Do ON" in May of that year garnered massive attention from both long-time fans and the "Reiwa generation" discovering them for the first time. 

The film captures RIP SLYME’s past and present, blending recent live performances and backstage footage with archival clips, a special New Year’s shrine visit filmed specifically for this project, and interviews with those close to the group. It is directed by Hiro Kanai, the acclaimed director behind hit TV dramas such as *The Confidence Man JP* and *HERO*, who shares a long-standing bond of trust with the members.
title:METライブビューイング2025-26 ジョルダーノ《アンドレア・シ:
The fourth production of the 2025–26 season of "MET Live Viewing," which brings the latest performances from New York’s Metropolitan Opera to movie theaters. This installment features "Andrea Chénier," an opera that uses the dynamic music of Verismo (realism) to depict the turmoil between a poet who met his end at the guillotine during the French Revolution, the countess who was his soulmate, and the revolutionary who loved her (scheduled to be recorded from the performance on December 13, 2025).

Paris, on the eve of the French Revolution. At the estate of the Count de Coigny, the servant Gérard harbors a growing resentment toward the establishment while secretly pining for the daughter of the house, Maddalena. At a party one evening, a guest named Andrea Chénier improvises a poem about love, striking a chord with both Maddalena and Gérard. Five years later, Gérard has risen to become a high-ranking official in the revolutionary government, yet he still cannot forget Maddalena. Now fallen from grace, Maddalena is reunited with Chénier, who has been branded an enemy of the revolution. Gérard arrests Chénier and exposes his raw desire for Maddalena, but by the time he realizes the depth of his own folly, Chénier has already been sentenced to death.
title:津田寛治に撮休はない:
A "new sensation" mystery featuring actor Kanji Tsuda—who has portrayed a vast array of roles across countless films and television dramas—playing himself as a lead for the first time. Kanji Tsuda is a face familiar to every film enthusiast. Leading a hectic life filled with filming, rehearsals, meetings, and events, the concept of a "day off from shooting" is non-existent to him. However, starting from a certain day, inexplicable events begin to occur around Tsuda, and he starts to suspect that someone is stalking him. As his mental state gradually deteriorates, Tsuda begins to lose his sense of self, eventually suffering from strange hallucinations. A shocking truth awaits him.

Tetsu Watanabe, Hiromi Iwasaki, Ryo Shinoda, Ren Komai, Shunya Itabashi, and film director Noboru Iguchi appear as themselves. The cast also includes Yuri Hirasawa as Tsuda’s daughter Sachi, Motoki Kobayashi as film director Sadahisa Kasanashi, Ryu Ichinose as popular young talent Rento Oshikida, and Yumiko Nakamura as Tsuda’s manager Seiko Kumi. The film is directed and written by Takayuki Kayano, known for "Danshari Paradise" and "Journey Beyond the Night."
title:REDLINE:
Based on an original story by Katsuhito Ishii (director of *Shark Skin Man and Peach Hip Girl* and *The Taste of Tea*), director Takeshi Koike—who helmed the "World Record" segment of the omnibus *The Animatrix*—brings this vision to life as an animated feature with a relentless commitment to hand-drawn techniques. The film depicts daredevil racers competing in "REDLINE," a race held once every five years to determine the fastest in the universe, brought to life through overwhelming visuals that took seven years and 100,000 hand-drawn frames to produce.

The cool protagonist JP, sporting a pompadour and leather jacket, is actually a shy and pure-hearted man. To win the heart of his first love, Sonoshee, he decides to enter "REDLINE," an anything-goes car race held in the military nation of "Roboworld." He enters the race in the ultimate machine customized by his childhood friend and genius mechanic, Frisbee, but the car has been rigged with a trap for match-fixing.

The film features a star-studded voice cast, including Takuya Kimura as JP, Yu Aoi as Sonoshee, and Tadanobu Asano as JP’s partner and mechanic, Frisbee. The screenplay was co-written by original creator Ishii alongside Yoji Enokido (*Neon Genesis Evangelion*) and Yoshiki Sakurai (*Ghost in the Shell: Stand Alone Complex*). Animation production was handled by Madhouse (*Summer Wars*).

Originally produced and released in 2010, the film saw a revival screening in February 2021 for its 10th anniversary. In December 2025, it will be screened as part of the special feature "Katsuhito Ishii World 30th Anniversary," celebrating the 30th anniversary of Ishii’s directorial debut.
title:陽なたのファーマーズ フクシマと希望: This documentary follows the journey of farmers in Fukushima Prefecture who, after being forced out of business by the nuclear accident, made a comeback and embarked on a new challenge: balancing agriculture and power generation through a method called "solar sharing." In 2012, Megumi Kondo, an organic farmer in Nihonmatsu City, Fukushima Prefecture, was driven to close his business by the disaster. He found a way forward through solar sharing (agrivoltaics)—generating solar power on active farmland—and in 2021, he began a new path as a farmer who also produces electricity. Because selling electricity provides farmers with a stable income, allowing them to strive for better crops and to recruit and train young successors, Kondo calls solar sharing a "new form of part-time farming." He continues this challenge alongside young farmers like Haru Tsukada, who was scouted by Kondo after graduating from an agricultural high school, and Yuki Kanno, who entered the field in his mid-30s. While depicting these young farmers and those seeking to learn about the intersection of agriculture and energy, the film offers a hopeful glimpse into the possibilities for solving social issues in Japan. The film is directed by Hiroyasu Obara (*The Judge Who Stopped Nuclear Power Plants and the Farmers Who Will Stop Them*). The theme song and narration are provided by Emi Shirasaki, vocalist of the musical group Shang Shang Typhoon and founder of the band "Tohoku 6-ken Roll Show!!," which she established following the Great East Japan Earthquake to honor the Tohoku region, including her native Yamagata Prefecture.
title:リアム・ギャラガー ライブ・アット・ネブワース2022: A live film documenting the solo outdoor concert performed by Liam Gallagher, the legendary voice of the British rock band Oasis, at Knebworth Park in 2022. Ever since Oasis drew 250,000 people there in 1996, Knebworth has been revered as the "holy ground" of the band. Having established himself as a solo artist following the group's 2009 split, Liam returned to the Knebworth stage for the first time in 26 years, performing for 170,000 ecstatic fans over two days. The film captures the 16-song set—featuring 10 Oasis anthems such as "Wonderwall," "Champagne Supernova," "Live Forever," and "Stand By Me"—alongside the liberating atmosphere and sense of pure joy unique to an outdoor festival. The encore also features a guest appearance by former Stone Roses guitarist John Squire.
tmdb:1505677: Written and directed by Kenji Yamauchi—known for films such as *Her Father, My Lover* and *At the Terrace*, as well as SoftBank’s "Shirato Family" commercial series—this social comedy follows a woman who provides nursing care on the first floor of her home while running an illegal adult entertainment business on the second floor while her husband is away. Yoko, who lives in Tokyo with her husband and cares for her father-in-law, harbors a deep dislike for a certain politician. Lately, on weekday afternoons while her husband is at work, she has been up to no good at home. Her husband remains oblivious, but the housewife next door seems to have noticed... Ami Cheng, who also starred in Yamauchi’s previous film *The Night Before the Wedding*, plays the protagonist Yoko. The cast also includes stage actress Eri Iwamoto, Takenori Kaneko (*Haruhara-san's Recorder*), and Kenji Iwaya (Netflix’s *Tokyo Swindlers*).
title:劇場版 ロッキンミュージカル シン・シャドウブラウン＆ブラックパイレ:
"Rockin' Musical: The Adventures of Shin Shadow Brown & Black Pirates" is a filmed theatrical release of the rock musical directed, staged, structured, and composed by musician Diamond Yukai.

The project traces its roots back to 1992, when Yukai conceived the story and music for "Shadow Brown & Black Pirates," a concept album released under the theme of "Rock x Adventure x Musical Theater." In 2025, 33 years after its debut, the original band members reunited to reconstruct the work into a rock musical that seamlessly blends music and drama. Capturing the performance held in Tokyo on March 15, 2025, this film brings to the big screen the exhilarating tale of a rockin' pirate band sailing through time and space.

Shadow Brown and his crew arrive at Jenny Island, a place where music has been banned. Though the island was once overflowing with song, all sound and smiles have since vanished, leaving the hearts of its people frozen in silence. After meeting a young girl who has lost her hope and light, Shadow Brown rises up to restore the joy of music to the world.
title:道草キッチン:
A human drama marking the first starring film role in 26 years for the multi-talented Yuri Nakae—active as an author, actress, and singer. The story follows a 50-year-old single woman who moves to Tokushima and begins to re-evaluate her way of life through encounters with local residents facing various circumstances, Vietnamese people living earnestly in Japan, and Vietnamese cuisine prepared with ingredients from the region's rich natural environment.

Ritsu Katsuragi is a 50-year-old woman running a small cafe in the city. Single and without any family or relatives, she had resolved to live out the rest of her days alone. However, her shop is forced to close due to urban redevelopment, and compounded by health issues, Ritsu finds herself at a loss, filled with anxiety about the future. It is then that a notice regarding an inheritance arrives from Yoshinogawa City in Tokushima Prefecture, prompting her to move there.

The film features a supporting cast including Hiroto Kanai (*SHOGUN*), Chika Araki (*Haruhara-san's Recorder*), Honoka Murakami (*Someone's Flowers*), singer Yoko Kon, and folk singer Masaji Otsuka. Mitsuhito Shiraha, known for *Sociology of a Philippine Pub Girl*, directs.

title:ムー×ベルジネピクチャーズ 未確認アニメ夜話: UMAs, the future, memory, psychology, spiritualism, the cosmos, unknown interference... This collection of animated works has caused a quiet stir within the editorial department of *Mu*, long-time observers of the world’s mysteries. The works of Shoji Kohara present questions that may truly exist on the flip side of reality, delivered in the form of stories. Unexplained voids. A sense of unease where meaning remains fluid. This program features a comprehensive screening of "liminal" animations—from past works to the latest—that resonate deeply with the interests of *Mu*. After the experience, the way you see the world may shift, if only slightly.
title:モンテ・クリスト伯: Alexandre Dumas’ 19th-century masterpiece "The Count of Monte Cristo," a cornerstone of revenge literature also known in Japan as "Gankutsuou," has been adapted into a live-action film in France. Young sailor Edmond Dantès is on the verge of a promotion to captain and an upcoming marriage when he is imprisoned for a crime he did not commit due to a sinister plot. Losing his will to live in the depths of despair, Dantès finds hope again after meeting an old priest planning an escape. The priest provides him with an education and reveals the existence of a hidden treasure belonging to the Knights Templar. Fourteen years after his incarceration, Dantès miraculously escapes, acquires the vast fortune, and finally sets his plan for revenge in motion. Appearing in Parisian high society as the mysterious millionaire "The Count of Monte Cristo," he skillfully closes in on the three men who stole his life. Pierre Niney (*Yves Saint Laurent*) stars, delivering an elegant and delicate performance as the ill-fated avenger Dantès. He is joined by Bastien Bouillon (*The Night of the 12th*), Anaïs Demoustier (*The New Girlfriend*), and Anamaria Vartolomei (*Happening*). At the 50th César Awards in 2025, the film received a year-high 14 nominations and won for Best Production Design and Best Costume Design.
