title:左ききのエレン（3話先行上映）:
The first three episodes of the TV anime *Left-Handed Eren*, set to premiere in April 2026, will receive a limited one-week theatrical screening ahead of the television broadcast. Based on the manga of the same name by Kappi, the story is set in the advertising industry and depicts a youth ensemble drama of creators. It centers on two individuals: Koichi, an ordinary man dreaming of becoming "something" while struggling with the limits of his own talent, and Eren, a solitary genius who suffers due to her overwhelming gift.

High school student Koichi Asakura, who aspires to attend an art university to become a designer, is one day struck by graffiti scrawled on an art museum wall. The artist is Eren Yamagishi, a left-handed high school girl who had suppressed her talent following a certain incident. Eventually, the two come to acknowledge one another through the act of "creating," and while Koichi sets out to become a designer and Eren a painter, their paths take unexpected turns...

Shoya Chiba voices the role of Koichi, and Yumi Uchiyama voices Eren Yamagishi. The series is directed by Toshimasa Suzuki (*Fafner in the Azure: Heaven and Earth*), with series composition by Taku Kishimoto (*Haikyu!!*). Takayuki Goto (*Ghost in the Shell: S.A.C. 2nd GIG*) provides the original animation character drafts, while Yuka Fukuchi and Akane Tamai (*Platinum End*) serve as character designers and chief animation directors. Animation production is a joint effort by Signal.MD and Production I.G.
tmdb:1634021:
The fourth installment of the "Our Recipe Book Series," a youth film project centered on the themes of community, food, and high school life. Set in Kiyose City, Tokyo, the film humorously depicts a summer spent by high school film club members with an alien they encounter by chance.

Hayate, a high school sophomore living in Kiyose, is a massive sci-fi fan. He secretly dreamed of filming an alien movie with his club, but had lost his nerve after his previous work was panned by critics. However, one day just before summer break, something crashes into the woods of Kiyose, and Hayate and his fellow film club members encounter a real alien there. Alongside this extraterrestrial visitor and a group of unique friends—including his handsome, movie-loving childhood friend Hinata; the cheerful and brainy "Bon-chan"; and the easygoing, music-loving Wakamizu—Hayate sets out to make his movie.

The film is directed by Masahide Ichii, known for "Haruchika: Bring on the Melody," "The Family of Typhoon," and "What to Do with My Husband: Oh My Charlie."
tmdb:1406829: A human drama depicting the inner lives of children wavering amidst the awakening of self, set against the backdrop of "Kodomo Kabuki" (Children's Kabuki) performed at the Nagahama Hikiyama Festival in Shiga Prefecture, a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage. Eleven-year-old Ibuki visits his father’s hometown of Nagahama for the first time with his Taiwanese mother, Yaohua, to deliver the remains of his father, Shuichi, who passed away a year ago. Nagahama is in the midst of preparations for its annual festival, and Ibuki is cast in the *onnagata* (female role) once performed by his father in the festival's Children's Kabuki. Struggling with the unfamiliar environment and the difficult phrasing of Kabuki, Ibuki becomes isolated, but he begins to slowly open up after meeting Hana, a young girl who feels a sense of dissonance

title:今は昔、栄養映画館の旅:
This documentary follows Akira Emoto and Tatsuki Nishimoto of the theatrical troupe Gekidan Tokyo Kandenzu as they tour independent cinemas across Japan with their staged reading of "Ima wa Mukashi, Eiyo Eigakan" (Once Upon a Time, the Nutrition Cinema).

Originally a masterpiece of absurdist theater by playwright Juichiro Takeuchi that premiered in 1983, the play was performed in its entirety as a staged reading by the duo of Emoto and Nishimoto in November 2024. It went on to have multiple encore performances at "Atelier Kandenzu," the troupe's rehearsal space and theater in Shimokitazawa, Tokyo. Because the play depicts the banter between a director and an assistant director waiting for a film's completion ceremony to begin in a movie theater, screenwriter and director Haruhiko Arai—who attended a performance—conceived the idea of staging the reading in actual cinemas. A month-long tour of 24 independent cinemas across the country took place from May 1 to 31, 2025, receiving critical acclaim from film and theater enthusiasts in every region.

This film captures their journey, showing Emoto, Nishimoto, and fellow members of Gekidan Tokyo Kandenzu as they travel to cinemas across Japan in a single van. Masaaki Takeda, who has served as an assistant director on works by directors Haruhiko Arai and Sang-il Lee, handled the direction, cinematography, and editing.
title:映画 冬のソナタ 日本特別版:
"Winter Sonata," the masterpiece drama that aired on Korea's KBS in 2002 and ignited the "Hallyu" boom in Japan after achieving high ratings on NHK in 2004, returns to theaters in a special 4K remastered edition exclusive to Japan. Conceived by director Yoon Seok-ho and the original production team as a gesture of gratitude for the enduring love from Japanese fans, this version features 4K remastered visuals and a soundtrack rearranged specifically for the cinema. Director Yoon oversaw the entire process, distilling the story down to its core—the "pure love" between the two protagonists—to create this definitive edition.

In high school, Joon-sang and Yu-jin were each other's first loves. However, Joon-sang is involved in a tragic car accident and never returns. Ten years later, Yu-jin, now running an interior design firm, encounters a man named Min-hyeong who looks exactly like her lost love.

Bae Yong-joon plays the dual roles of Joon-sang and Min-hyeong, starring alongside Choi Ji-woo as the heroine.
title:ムー×ベルジネピクチャーズ 未確認アニメ夜話:
An omnibus collection of short animations by Director Seiji Ohara, who has earned high acclaim both in Japan and abroad for his hand-drawn works and unique worldview. Produced between 2011 and 2025 in collaboration with "Mu"—a cultural magazine dedicated to exploring the mysterious and the unknown—the collection features six films centered on enigmatic themes.

The lineup consists of: "Concrete
title:みつかるまで:
Directed by Takusho Tsunemoto, known for "A Pale Woman," this medium-length film was shot on 16mm as a 2002 practical project for the Film School of Tokyo’s advanced course. It depicts the encounter between a female painter who can no longer paint and a man drawn to her, following the fate that awaits them both. 

Yoshimi, a painter who once held high ideals, lost her sense of self after a professional setback and now spends her days pickpocketing on trains. A mysterious young man named Tetsushi appears before her, telling her that the sight of her running through the city at night was like a shooting star. From then on, he begins to follow her, always wearing an affable smile. 

Yuka Itaya stars as the protagonist Yoshimi, with Kenji Mizuhashi playing Tetsushi, a young man whose friendly grin hides a deep-seated fragility. Although it has primarily been shown at film festivals and special screenings, the film will receive its standalone theatrical release at Cinema Ris in Jimbocho, Tokyo, in 2026—24 years after its production.
title:あかねの一週間: Featured in the "OP PICTURES+ Festival 2025" (November 22 – December 4, 2025 / Theatre Shinjuku, Tokyo).
tmdb:1584762:
This documentary explores "Barefoot Gen," the manga created by Keiji Nakazawa based on his own experiences as an atomic bomb survivor, which has long been read as a powerful plea for anti-war and anti-nuclear causes. The story follows the young protagonist, Gen, who survives the bombing but loses his family, depicting his resilient struggle through poverty and prejudice.

In the half-century since its serialization began in Weekly Shonen Jump in 1973, the work has been translated into 25 languages and read across the globe. However, in recent years, it has become the subject of intense debate; some have called for restrictions on its access in school libraries, citing "extreme depictions" or the "instilling of incorrect historical perceptions," and it has even been removed from Hiroshima City’s peace education materials.

Why does "Barefoot Gen" continue to ignite such passion in people? By examining the work from its inception to the present day, this film reflects the anger, sadness, and kindness that permeate our world. A cinematic adaptation of the September 2024 BS12 Special "The Transmission of 'Barefoot Gen': Those Who Pass on the Atomic Bomb Manga," the film is directed by Masanori Komiyama, a veteran of numerous documentary programs making his feature debut. Arata Oshima (Kagawa 1st District, The Day of the State Funeral) and Aki Maeda (NO ELECTION, NO LIFE) serve as co-producers.
title:父と家族とわたしのこと:
A documentary that quietly observes people confronting inherited pain through the lens of war trauma. In recent years, the fact that many returning soldiers suffered from severe PTSD has increasingly come to light. The deep scars etched into their hearts are sometimes passed down to children and grandchildren in the form of domestic violence or addiction, leading to the breakdown of family relationships. 

The film follows three individuals: a woman who grew up suffering severe abuse from her father and went on to abuse her own daughter; a man whose childhood memories of his veteran father’s verbal abuse toward his mother left deep scars that eventually led to violence against his own wife; and a woman who developed complex PTSD due to abuse from her mother—who was devoted to a new religious movement—and who continues to struggle with her relationship with her own daughter. As the investigation delves into the roots of the "difficulty in living" shared by these three, a common thread emerges: their fathers or grandfathers all served in the war. 

The film is directed by Hyoma Shimada, who has documented individuals striving to stand on their own feet while being at the mercy of massive forces like war and national division in works such as "I'm Just Going to North Korea for a Bit" and "Live, Live, Live." The music was composed by Takashi Watanabe, known for "The Great Passage."
title:『雑居家族』 ＊デジタル上映: Synopsis: Depicts the affection-filled daily life of a female writer living under one roof with children and lodgers to whom she is not related by blood. This film is an adaptation of a novel based on the personal experiences of the original author, Sakae Tsuboi.
title:『夜の 鴎 』: Synopsis: An unconventional melodrama depicting a woman who, having lost her loved ones one after another, nevertheless strives to live in search of love. Directed by the renowned actor Saburi from an original screenplay by Tanaka.
title:『にっぽんのお婆ぁちゃん』: Synopsis: The comical odyssey of elderly women with checkered pasts who meet in Asakusa. A social comedy addressing the issues of the elderly, this is a tour de force that Mizuki brought to the screen after co-founding a production company with the director and others.
tmdb:1559185:
Shunsuke Michieda (Naniwa Danshi) and director Takahiro Miki, who previously collaborated on "Even If This Love Disappears from the World Tonight," reunite for this love story based on the novel of the same name by Misaki Ichijo, the author of the original work. The film depicts a 10-year romance between a man and a woman who are drawn to each other through their time spent creating music together, even as they are buffeted by the whims of fate.

Haruto Mizushima is a high school student living an unremarkable life with a secret hobby of writing poetry. His secret is discovered by his classmate, Ayane Tosaka. While Ayane possesses a singing voice that captivates all who hear it, she struggles with developmental dyslexia, making it difficult for her to read and write. When Ayane asks Haruto to write lyrics for her, the two begin to grow closer while spending time alone in a clubroom after school making songs together, but...

Meru Nukumi, appearing alongside Michieda for the first time, plays the heroine. She makes her debut performance as a singer and guitarist after nearly a year of vocal training and guitar lessons. The screenplay is penned by Tomoko Yoshida ("Let Me Eat Your Pancreas"), with music production by Seiji Kameda ("Even If This Love Disappears from the World Tonight," "Threads - Our Tapestry of Love").
tmdb:1653724:
Set in Shinjuku’s Kabukicho district following the closure of the "Toyoko" area, this suspenseful drama follows four displaced youths over the course of a single night as their fates collide in a desperate escape. Marking the feature film debut of 24-year-old rising talent Ren Akiba, the film is a feature-length adaptation of his own short film which won the Grand Prix at the 2nd Tokyo Independent Film Festival. Akiba penned the original screenplay based on his own personal experiences in Kabukicho.

Asuka, a high school student who feels she has no place at home or school, travels to Kabukicho drawn by "Tokyo Escape," an autobiographical web novel written by a girl living in Toyoko. There, she happens to meet the author, Hiyori, and the two form an immediate bond. Asuka is introduced to Edo, a protector of the drifters in Toyoko, and Merio, a leader among the local youth. However, after attending a meeting led by Merio and witnessing a shocking reality, Asuka grabs Hiyori’s hand and flees. Their escape earns them the ire of a "han-gure" criminal group, leaving them hunted. Meanwhile, Edo fights to preserve the youths' sanctuary, while Merio makes increasingly dangerous choices in his pursuit of the girls. As the police become involved, the situation spirals toward a rapid climax.

The film stars Rio Teramoto as Asuka, Akana Ikeda as Hiyori, Keito Tsuna as Edo, and Nao Takahashi as Merio, supported by Yuya Matsuura, Motoki Fukami, and Honami Sato. Michihito Fujii, director of *The Strangers* and *The Journalist*, serves as executive producer.
title:韓国ミュージカル ON SCREEN「モーツァルト！」:
One of the entries in the "Korean Musical ON SCREEN" cinema series, which brings masterpieces of Korean musical theater to the big screen. This screening features the 2020 production of "Mozart!" starring singer and actor Kim Junsu as the composer, accompanied by special footage including interviews with the cast and crew and behind-the-scenes glimpses.

Hailed as a "miracle child," Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart lives in the world of music alongside his father and sister. As a young man, he leaves his hometown for Vienna to test his talents in the wider world. While he marries Constanze, a daughter of the Weber family where he resided, and works tirelessly, the overwhelming brilliance of his childhood eventually fades, leading him into a desolate life. Always by his side is Amadé, the "incarnation of talent," watching him with cold eyes.

The renowned duo of Michael Kunze (book and lyrics) and Sylvester Levay (music and arrangements) explores Mozart's life, shining a spotlight on both the light and shadow of his existence. Since its 1999 premiere in Vienna, the musical has gained worldwide acclaim. It debuted in South Korea in 2010, becoming a massive hit that drew approximately 100,000 spectators in just one month.
title:おしり前マン 復活のおしり前帝国: A feature-length animated film by Takashi Taniguchi—who gained fame for his unique independent animations on YouTube—based on his original work, "Oshiri Mae Man." Maejiri, a man whose buttocks are located on the front of his body, is an ordinary salaryman, but his true identity is "Oshiri Mae Man," a hero who protects the peace of the city. One day, a drug called "Shiri-maenare" that moves people's buttocks to the front begins circulating, plunging the town into unprecedented chaos. While investigating the cause, Oshiri Mae Man tracks it back to a certain pharmaceutical company. There, he discovers a document titled "Oshiri Mae Empire Resurrection Plan" and learns of an organization plotting to revive the legendary "Oshiri Mae Empire" to rule over the people. Realizing the gravity of the situation, Oshiri Mae Man resolves to train under his former master, "Oshiri Mae Legend" (the previous Oshiri Mae Man). Just as with his short films, Taniguchi personally handles the majority of the production for this feature-length version, including direction, screenplay, animation, voice acting, and music.

Following his passing on January 15 last year (2025), director David Lynch—often hailed as the "King of Cult" for his one-of-a-kind style and beloved by a fervent global fanbase—will be honored with an all-night screening event titled "David Lynch Night," featuring three of his iconic works in a single night.

Mulholland Drive (4K Restored Edition)
Mulholland Drive: a midnight mountain road shrouded in darkness. One night, a car crash occurs, leaving a beautiful woman as the sole survivor, though she has lost her memory. She hides in the home of a famous actress while the owner is away, only to be discovered by Betty, an aspiring young actress. Lacking any memory of her identity, the woman impulsively calls herself Rita...
2001 | USA | 146 min (PG12)
Director/Screenplay: David Lynch
Cast: Naomi Watts, Laura Harring, Ann Miller, Justin Theroux, Dan Hedaya, Mark Pellegrino, Brian Beacock, et al.

Twin Peaks: Fire Walk with Me (4K)
The theatrical film of the U.S. TV series that sparked a global phenomenon. It serves as a prequel to the serial murder mystery set in the rural town of Twin Peaks. "Twin Peaks" became a cultural sensation in Japan, even entering the popular lexicon, and was a massive hit with 2 billion yen in distribution revenue.
1992 | USA | 135 min
Director/Screenplay: David Lynch
Cast: Sheryl Lee, Ray Wise, Dana Ashbrook, Kyle MacLachlan, David Bowie, Kiefer Sutherland

Lost Highway (4K)
Fred, a saxophonist, lives an ordinary life with his wife until one day, a mysterious videotape arrives. It contains footage of his wife’s dismembered body, leading to his imprisonment... A terrifying "Lynchian world" where reality dissolves, this was his seventh feature film and his first in five years.
1997 | USA | 135 min
Director/Screenplay: David Lynch | Screenplay: Barry Gifford
Cast: Bill Pullman, Patricia Arquette, Balthazar Getty, Robert Blake, Robert Loggia, Jack Nance
title:あのとき: Amidst a landscape of lush nature, a woman walks with single-minded intensity toward a destination. When she pauses and closes her eyes, the days spent with her daughter return to her in vivid detail. Interweaving documentary footage of the director raising her own child with a fictional narrative, this hybrid film is a quiet meditation on what it means to live in the now.
title:ジーンシアタースタジアム: Geneheart Co., Ltd. operates GeneTheater, a Japanese subscription service specializing in short films, and the mini-theater and indie film information site Nihon Eiga Online, alongside its theatrical distribution business. Launched in October 2025, GeneTheater Stadium is a program that screens three independent short films together for a limited one-week engagement. Under the banner "Movies aren't just for watching. It's time to nurture them!", the project implements a new form of film screening centered on three keywords: audience participation, discovering your "oshi" (favorites), and short films.
title:100,000年後の安全: Once the waste reaches a certain level, the facility will be sealed, never to be opened again. But who can guarantee that? Is there a way to reliably warn the people living there 100,000 years from now of the danger? They might mistake it for ruins or tombs of our era, or a place where treasures are hidden. Will those people of the future even understand
title:歴史は女で作られる: Max Ophüls, director of *La Ronde* (1950), helms this epic depicting the romantic escapades of the real-life 19th-century beauty and dancer Lola Montès, brought to life through the brilliant hues of Technicolor and the grand scale of CinemaScope. Although it was one of the most expensive French productions of 1956, it is famously known as a "cursed masterpiece" after the studio re-edited the film against the director’s wishes, sparking controversy and leading to its commercial failure. In 19th-century Paris, after a troubled childhood, Lola Montès becomes a circus dancer and gains notoriety through a series of high-profile affairs. Eventually, she finds her way to Bavaria, where she captivates King Ludwig I. The film was revived in 2011 with a digitally remastered version.
title:GEMNIBUS vol.2: The second installment of "GEMNIBUS," an omnibus film featuring up-and-coming directors, created as part of Toho's talent support project "GEMSTONE Creative Label." The collection consists of six films: "Blue Bird," a road movie by photographer and director Sara Masuda, who explores her own impulses and struggles with the act of "capturing images" through overwhelming visual beauty set against the vast nature of Hokkaido; "Influencer Ghost," a stylish creature horror film by director Masaki Nishiyama (noted for his short "Sumahora!") who teams up with Shoichiro Sato, a VFX artist for "Godzilla Minus One"; "The Faceless City," an SF psychological horror by director Riko Murakami (of "MIRRORLIAR FILMS") that vividly depicts a near-future dystopia based on the Grand Prize winner of the "Toho x World Maker Short Film Contest" co-hosted by Shueisha’s "Shonen Jump+" and Toho; "Sonic Beat," a youth story by director Shunta Seki (highly acclaimed for his graduation film "Bowl Meets Girl") who mirrors his own journey into his first commercial work through a protagonist in a high school track club; "You Cannot Be Serious!", a comedy by award-winning director Satsuki Okawa that portrays the struggles of a woman with an unexpected pregnancy in a realistic yet pop style; and "If We Are to Be Born," an animated work by director Asuka Dokai that uses the theme of "ten months and ten days" to fantastically chronicle twins waking up on a mysterious planet and embarking on a journey to meet the mother they have never seen.
title:レオ ブラッディ・スウィート: Director Lokesh Kanagaraj (Kaithi, Vikram) reunites with superstar Vijay following their collaboration on Master for this high-octane crime action film. In the quiet North Indian town of Theog, Parthiban, a Tamil animal rescuer and cafe owner, lives a peaceful life with his wife and two children. However, their tranquility is shattered when a gang led by Shanmugam rampages through the town and breaks into his cafe. Though Parthiban initially tries to settle the matter by handing over the cash, he is forced to act when his daughter and female staff are threatened, taking down five attackers in an instant with extraordinary physical prowess. As the incident makes national headlines, various underworld factions begin to target him—including mysterious men who insist on calling him "Leo." Trisha Krishnan (Ponniyin Selvan: I & II) stars as Parthiban's wife, with Sanjay Dutt (K.G.F: Chapter 2, PK) appearing as the gangster hunting for Leo.
title:ゲキ×シネ「紅鬼物語」:
"Beni-oni Monogatari," performed in Tokyo and Osaka in early summer 2025 as Gekidan Shinkansen's 45th-anniversary production, has been adapted into a "Geki×Cine" film, allowing audiences to experience the troupe's stage performance on the big screen. Starring Rei Yuzuka—the former Flower Troupe top star making her first stage appearance since leaving the Takarazuka Revue—this Shinkansen-style "fairy tale" unfolds, depicting the destiny of a woman born as an ogre who fell in love with a human.

Once upon a time, long, long ago. It was an era when ogres appeared in the capital and terrorized the people. Sakagami Kinnosuke, a vassal of the nobleman Minamoto no Ao, is attacked by an ogre but manages to fight back and repel it. When he reports this to Ao, fellow vassal Usui Shima suggests that the mysterious disappearance of Ao's wife might also be the work of an ogre. One morning ten years ago, Ao's wife Beniko and daughter Fuji vanished without a trace, leaving behind footprints in the garden that appeared to belong to an ogre. Believing even now that Beniko and Fuji are still alive, Ao resolves to find the ogres' stronghold and bring them back, heading to the Onmyoji Abe no Hechima with Kinnosuke and the others. Meanwhile
title:ゾンビ 1/2 Right Side of the Living D:
A unique zombie comedy set in a post-pandemic world, following the new chaos sparked by a protagonist living as the world's only "half-zombie" who retained his sanity.

Ryoma Shingu, an ordinary office worker, is the sole remaining zombie after humanity triumphed over the undead threat. As a "half-zombie" who can maintain his reason despite being bitten, he has been living a quiet, mundane life accepted by society. Ryoma wants nothing more than to live peacefully without making waves, but when he accidentally bites Uzame, a college intern at his company, a new zombie pandemic erupts. Although Ryoma knows he must stop the chaos, his deep-seated introverted nature gets in the way, making it difficult for him to take action...

Sojiro Yoshimura of "PARALLEL" stars as Ryoma, alongside Seina Nakata, Koki Tanaka (SUPER★DRAGON), Shunsuke Daito, and Kiyobumi Kaneko. The screenplay is written by Moral, known for "Crayon Shin-chan the Movie: Our Dinosaur Diary" and the anime "Hiroshi Nohara's Lunch Style." The film was produced as the second installment of "M CINEMA," a project by production company Media Mix Japan (MMJ) that provides opportunities for young employees in their 20s and 30s to plan and produce theatrical films.
title:365DAYs＋2彷徨う大人たち:
In the documentary "365DAYs+," director Hiroyuki Kitaura chronicled the year leading up to his retirement. This sequel serves as a personal record of his daily life and inner struggles starting from his second year after leaving the workforce. After 36 years as a corporate "salaryman," Kitaura transitioned into life as a househusband and documentary filmmaker. His first year of retirement was a whirlwind of activity, from visiting the public employment office to enrolling in graduate school.

By his second year, as the initial dust settled, Kitaura had finally attained the "free and unconstrained life" he had dreamed of during his corporate days. Yet, he noticed a faint, lingering sense of unease beginning to stir deep within him. To uncover the source of this feeling, he once again began filming with his smartphone. Through his graduate studies, travels across Japan and Asia, and interviews with friends and former colleagues, he searches for the true nature of this restlessness. During a month-and-a-half stay in Manila to obtain a Philippine retirement visa, Kitaura spent his time reflecting while staring at his hotel ceiling, eventually arriving at a conclusion he presents as "My Personal Formula for Happiness."

Like its predecessor, the film is constructed from a vast archive of footage captured daily on a smartphone. It offers a raw look at the reality of life for a retiree while exploring the fundamental meaning of "working" and "continuing to work" through candid interviews.
tmdb:1607600:
A youth music film reuniting director Tomorowo Taguchi and screenwriter Kankuro Kudo of *Iden & Tity*. Based on the autobiographical essay *Street Kingdom* by photographer Yuichi Jibiki—who served as the group's photographer and manager—the film depicts the "Tokyo Rockers," a movement of young people who forged Japan's first homegrown punk rock scene.

In 1978, Yuichi, a young photographer who moved to Tokyo driven by the sounds of the Sex Pistols on the radio, visits a live house after discovering a small rock zine called *Rockin' Doll*. There, he finds a space overflowing with raw energy where music, bands, and audiences are free from all restraint. Blown away by a performance from the band TOKAGE, led by vocalist Momo, Yuichi captures the moment in a frenzy of shutter clicks.

Officially hired as their photographer, Yuichi grows closer to the band. Their music soon ignites a fire among the youth, evolving into the "Tokyo Rockers" movement that would redefine the landscape of Japanese rock.

Kazunobu Mineta stars as Yuichi alongside Ryuya Wakaba as Momo. The ensemble cast includes Riho Yoshioka, Taiga Nakano, Shotaro Mamiya, Nao Omori, Shido Nakamura, and Sena Nakajima. Yoshihide Otomo (*Iden & Tity*) provides the film's music.
title:劇場版 盗るな撮れ 罪と少年とケーブルTV: The Movie: Don't Steal, Shoot – Sin, Boys, and the Cell... View related works: Minding the Gap, FLEE, The Biggest Little Farm, The Act of Killing, Navalny, Citizenfour, Assassins, Documentary of Baby Assassins, The Yokai's Grandson, The Unnameable Dance.
title:Ryuichi Sakamoto | Trio Tour 2012:
World-renowned musician Ryuichi Sakamoto’s "Trio Tour 2012"—a concert series performed in a trio arrangement of piano, cello, and violin—comes to the big screen. In 1996, Sakamoto released the album *1996*, a work that achieved peak musical expression through a simple trio configuration, leading to a highly acclaimed world tour. In 2012, he produced the long-awaited trio album *THREE* and embarked on a 12-show tour of Japan and South Korea alongside long-time collaborator Jaques Morelenbaum (cello) and Judy Kang (violin), who was selected through an online audition. 

The repertoire includes classics from his YMO days, iconic film scores such as "Merry Christmas Mr. Lawrence" and "The Last Emperor," and the new piece "Shizen no Koe." Furthermore, reflecting the tour’s visit to the Tohoku region just one year after the Great East Japan Earthquake, the performance also features the charity song "Kizuna World" and "Yae no Sakura," the main theme for the Taiga drama set in Fukushima. This film captures the complete performance at Tokyo’s Akasaka ACT Theater, a show where a sense of tension and intimacy quietly coexisted. The audio has been specially reconstructed for the cinema to faithfully recreate the atmosphere of the venue to the fullest extent.

tmdb:1505896: Three souls adrift in loss: a young man who has lost his lover, a radio personality mourning his wife, and a woman burdened by a mysterious memory disorder and a longing for death. Before them appears a mysterious woman named Tsuta. Though she carries herself with a cheerful air, she exudes a faint scent of death, acting as if she has known them all along. Soon, the three must come face to face with their own life and mortality.
title:『森に聴く Listen to the Forest』舞台挨拶:
In Japan, where 70% of the land is covered by forest, we embark on a journey to discover the true nature of the woods that surround us. From the foothills of Mt. Oakan in Hokkaido to Naruko in Miyagi, Hita in Oita, Mt. Ichifusa in Kumamoto, and Iriomote Island in Okinawa, this nature documentary travels across the archipelago with leading researchers. Venturing deep into forests of giant trees that have survived far longer than humanity, the film explores the mysteries of their intelligence. What is the world that these forests of multi-species coexistence—a realm beyond human imagination—are trying to tell us?

Tetsujiro Yamagami, the producer behind such films as *Village of Dreams*, *All Around Us*, and *The Miharashi Generation*, reflects on his own life as he makes his directorial debut. He is joined by Yuki Imai, a filmmaker who continues to explore the relationship between humanity and nature in works like *Heguru*, as they tackle this new theme together. Within the flow of eternal time, how do nature and humans connect, and where are we headed from here?

Her hope for a fresh start is short-lived when she discovers serious structural defects in her unit. The real estate company refuses
title:ライフテープ:
This is the first documentary from director and actor Ryo Anraku, who has previously crafted feature films based on his own experiences, such as *Halfway Through a Dream* and *Dance of One*. The film tenderly captures the precious daily lives of Anraku’s close friend, street dancer and artist Ryuichi, his wife Ayaka, their son Haku—who lives with Menkes disease, a rare condition said to affect only one in 120,000 people—and their cat, Figaro.

In October 2022, after being asked by his childhood friend Ryuichi to "film my family," Anraku began documenting their lives. Their newborn son, Haku, has Menkes disease, a congenital metabolic disorder caused by a copper deficiency that leads to various health complications. While the life shared by the family and Figaro is filled with smiles, Ayaka’s diary—written between Haku's birth and his diagnosis—contains the raw, poignant words of a couple struggling to accept their reality. As they continue to weave their daily lives while confronting inescapable loneliness and anxiety, they reach a major turning point: the decision for Haku to undergo a tracheostomy. This theatrical version has been re-edited from the edition screened at the 16th Za-Koenji Documentary Festival in 2025.
title:山人（やまんど） 縄文の響きが木霊する: Director Masaki Haramura, who has produced numerous documentaries on the theme of agriculture, presents the final installment of his "Yamazato Trilogy," following "Young People Aim for Mountain Villages" and "Mountain Villages Were a Sustainable World." The film follows Toichi Kanke, born in 1953, who lives in a remote village in the Oku-Aizu region of Fukushima Prefecture. As a "Yamando" (mountain person) with an intimate knowledge of the wilderness, he enters the mountains throughout the four seasons, living off their bounty without destroying the natural environment. His way of life follows a code of conduct passed down since the Jomon period, offering clues for how we might realize a sustainable future. In spring, when a variety of wild plants sprout, he ensures a continuous harvest for years to come by never picking them all. A craftsman of traditional household goods made from mountain plants, Kanke is a successor to the ancestral wisdom of utilizing precious resources without exhausting them, and he speaks of sensing the presence of the divine within the mountains. While Kanke maintains that "hunting is culture," he is deeply pained by the indiscriminate killing of bears for pest control; fearing their potential extinction, he has ceased hunting them deep in the mountains. Before each hunting season begins, he never fails to perform memorial services for the wildlife. Through the life of Kanke, embraced by the blessings of the mountains, the film brings to light a vital message that modern society is losing sight of.
title:MAMMON:
A crime suspense drama centered on the theme of "yami baito" (illegal part-time jobs)—a growing social issue in recent years—depicting ordinary young people who, despite harboring dreams and ideals, struggle with the gap between their aspirations and reality, falling into an irreversible descent into darkness triggered by seemingly trivial choices.

Reiji, an aspiring photographer working part-time in Tokyo, hears that his old friend Yuya has found a job, and the two meet up with another friend, Mocchan. Reunited after a long time, they celebrate Yuya’s employment. However, in contrast to Reiji and Yuya, who are on the verge of grasping their hopes, Mocchan has already turned to a life of crime. Six months later, Yuya is laid off due to the COVID-19 pandemic and ends up getting his hands dirty in criminal activity alongside Mocchan. Meanwhile, Reiji has a precious girlfriend named Mina, but he is backed into a corner by a certain incident and begins to spiral toward his own ruin.

The film was planned and produced by "Gamushara Club," a creator team consisting of actors Yamato Tokuhiro, Kai Morishita, and Yuto Kawasaki, who have gained attention for their vertical short dramas. Tokuhiro stars as Reiji, Morishita as Mocchan, and Kawasaki as Yuya. Jita Matsunami served as director and screenwriter.
title:鍵から抜け出した女: An adventure-action romance feature film adaptation of the short story of the same name by Juza Unno, who published numerous science fiction novels from the pre-war to post-war eras and is known as the "father of Japanese SF." After losing her father, Haruko Homura travels to Kofujima, the island where his villa remains. There, Haruko learns that her father was a detective and encounters Junichi Kitagawa, an escapee from a facility on the island. This meeting draws Haruko into a mysterious incident involving her late father. The protagonist, Haruko, is played by singer-songwriter Saki in her first solo lead role in a feature film; she also performs the theme song. The cast includes Soji Arai (Apple TV’s *Pachinko*, NHK special drama *Queen of Mars*), Yuki Hashiguchi (*The First Job*), and Shiro Sano, an avid reader of Unno’s work. The film is written and directed by Takumi Takashina, who earned three consecutive PFF Award selections at the Pia Film Festival and makes his theatrical debut with this film.
title:METライブビューイング2025-26 ベッリーニ《清教徒》: The fifth production of the 2025–26 season of "MET Live Viewing," which broadcasts the latest performances from the Metropolitan Opera (MET) in New York to movie theaters. This installment features *I Puritani*, the final work of Bellini, who died at the young age of 33. A pinnacle of bel canto opera, it depicts the course of a romance
title:TRUE KYOHEI SAKAGUCHI SHOW 坂口恭平生活:
A documentary capturing the life and creative output of Kyohei Sakaguchi, a multi-disciplinary artist active as a writer, painter, musician, and architect. Born in Kumamoto Prefecture in 1978, Sakaguchi is known for numerous works, including *0-Yen House*, a photo book documenting the dwellings of the homeless, and *How to Build an Independent State*, which reexamines the role of government and the nation. In 2011, spurred by a lack of trust in the government’s response to the Great East Japan Earthquake, Sakaguchi declared the establishment of a "New Government" in his hometown of Kumamoto and began accepting evacuees at his base, "Zero Center." Since 2012, he has made his personal phone number public to continue "Inocchi no Denwa" (Telephone of Life), a telephone consultation service for those struggling with suicidal ideation. Sakaguchi is also open about his own experience with bipolar disorder, at times sinking into deep depression to confront himself.

New director Yuki Komiya, making his feature debut, began following Sakaguchi over a long period after visiting a solo exhibition and filming a live performance as an audience member. The film records Sakaguchi's daily life in Kumamoto, as well as his exhibitions and events across the country. Even as filming was interrupted when Sakaguchi fell into a deep depression during production, the documentary vividly captures the process of him giving birth to new words while facing a fundamental "loneliness" he has carried since before his childhood. Director Komiya personally edited footage previously released on YouTube to complete this theatrical version.
tmdb:441660: Sengoku once took the world by storm with performances using his ability to read residual thoughts, but the mental toll eventually drove him to retire from show business and seek a quiet life. One day, a high school pianist named Ami approaches him with a request for the "Mighties" to find her missing music teacher, Yukie. Though initially reluctant, Sengoku eventually teams up with his former partner, Mighty Maruyama, to pursue the mystery of the case.
title:テレビショッピングの女王 青池春香の事件チャンネル:
The first installment in the "2-Hour Suspense THE MOVIE" series, this film is inspired by the "2-hour suspense drama"—a genre produced and broadcast by commercial networks from the Showa to Heisei eras that became an iconic staple of Japanese television culture beloved by audiences. Starring Yuko Natori, a veteran lead of numerous 2-hour suspense dramas, and comedian Tomochika, the story follows TV shopping presenters as they pursue the truth behind a murder that occurs during a live broadcast.

Haruka Aoike, a legendary navigator and charismatic buyer boasting the highest sales in TV shopping history, and Yoshie Nagano, the "Queen of Live Demonstrations" whose featured products fly off the shelves, find themselves embroiled in a murder case during a live sales showdown. Though the two are clashing rivals, they unite under the common goal of uncovering the truth, using their wit and insight to close in on the heart of the mystery. Natori plays the legendary Haruka while Tomochika portrays the demonstration queen Yoshie, showcasing a perfectly synchronized and witty rapport.
title:揺さぶられる正義: A documentary based on years of reporting by a lawyer-journalist on Shaken Baby Syndrome (SBS) cases, which have led to numerous wrongful convictions. Following the clash of competing "justices," the film questions the state of the judiciary and the media.
title:フジコ・ヘミング 永遠の音色【三回忌追悼上映】: Fuzjko Hemming passed away on April 21, 2024, at the age of 92. Renowned as the "Pianist of the Soul," she captured the hearts of audiences worldwide with her deeply emotive performances. This film offers the ultimate big-screen experience of Fuzjko’s artistry, featuring her breathtaking rendition of "La Campanella," numerous classical masterpieces, and original works by contemporary composers—including previously unreleased footage. What new truths will emerge from her interview clips, illustrated diaries, and the testimonies of her family, including her brother Wolf and half-sister Eva, whose existence is revealed in this work? Having overcome immense hardships to finally achieve recognition and become a beloved pianist in her 60s, her tumultuous yet brilliant life continues to inspire courage in us all. The film is narrated by actress Miho Kanno.
